positionName: Affiliate Operations Coordinator (HR and Administrative Coordinator) - Brooklyn
company: Physician Affiliate Group of New York
description:
Physician Affiliate Group of New York, P.C. (PAGNY), a multispecialty medical practice group, staffs several municipal hospitals and clinics within New York City through an affiliation agreement with NYC Health + Hospitals. PAGNY’s corporate staff provides administrative, practice management, and human resources services in support of its nearly 4,000 clinical providers.

The mission of NYC Health + Hospitals is to extend equally to all New Yorkers, regardless of the ability to pay, comprehensive health services of the highest quality in an atmosphere of humane care, dignity, and respect. Their Values are built on a foundation of social and racial equity and has established the ICARE standards for all staff.

NYC Health + Hospitals is the nation’s largest municipal health care delivery system in the United States. Dedicated to providing the highest quality health care services to all New Yorkers with compassion, dignity and respect, and regardless of immigration status or ability to pay.

The Affiliate Operations Coordinator, for locations in the Brooklyn Region, is responsible for all aspects of administrative support, employee file maintenance, and ensuring employee files are compliant and kept to required standards. Acting as a backup for the HR team, the Affiliate Operations Coordinator will field employee questions and concerns for follow-up and is expected to serve as the outward facing team member for HR Department.

Opportunity Details:

Assists Human Resources team with onboarding, continuing medical education (CME) credits tracking, and other related transactions, ensuring all required documents are uploaded to HRIS for employee personnel files.
Executes frequent and ongoing file audits, focusing on identifying and following up on any missing documents and expired certifications. Assists with scheduled and unscheduled audits.
Manages compliance with mandatory and non-mandatory training, performance assessments, professional licensure, and aptitude exams.
Maintains detailed calendars and prioritizes meeting requests and logistics.
Supports on-site leadership with taking meeting minutes and prepares correspondence and financial reports.
Oversees conference room scheduling and any related catering orders and supplies for special events.
Operates and maintains standard office inventory. Reports any malfunctioning equipment, maintenance, or housekeeping issues to site leader, vendors, or building management and notifies staff, when necessary.
Receives deliveries, sorts and distributes incoming mail, and organizes outgoing courier deliveries and documents.
Prepares and reviews invoices and expense reports in a timely fashion.
Associate degree preferred, or equivalent combination of education and/or training
At least 2-4 years’ experience in a healthcare setting
Previous experience with managing high volume
Proven ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with all levels of staff
Proficient use of Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel) required
High attention to detail with the ability prioritize tasks in a deadline driven environment
Consistently display the highest degree of integrity, professionalism, and discretion
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills
On site, in-office presence required

Benefits include:

401(k) Company Contribution (subject to IRS contribution limits):
Employees are immediately vested in a 3% company contribution of base earnings. No employee match is required.
After one year of service, employees receive an additional 7% company contribution of base earnings. No employee match is required.
Generous Annual Paid Time Off (PTO): Vacation and Holiday.
Medical, Prescription, and Dental Coverage: Top-tier plans with employee contributions significantly below market rates.
Life Insurance and Accidental Death and Dismemberment (AD&D) Coverage: Equal to 3x your salary (up to a maximum of $250,000) provided at no cost to you.
Additional employee-paid Voluntary Life and AD&D coverage is available for you and your family.
Healthcare and Dependent Care Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs).
Pre-tax employee-paid contributions for commuting expenses.
Annual Salary = $45,000* - $55,000*

Physician Affiliate Group of New York, P.C. (PAGNY) mission is to provide accountable, responsive, quality care with the highest degree of sensitivity to the needs of the diverse population that lives in our New York community. PAGNY is one of the largest physician groups in the country and directly employs nearly 4,000 physicians and allied health professionals who provide services to NYC Health + Hospitals, the largest municipal health care system in the nation serving more than a million New Yorkers annually. Our providers are highly skilled professionals with outstanding credentials who deliver the highest level of quality healthcare to patients throughout New York City.

Physician Affiliate Group of New York, P.C. (PAGNY) is an equal opportunity employer committed to equitable hiring practices and a supportive workplace. All candidates are considered based on their individual qualifications, potential, and experience. To learn more about our culture and ongoing workplace practices, please visit our Workplace Culture | PAGNY page.

*Salary Disclosure Information:

The salary listed for this position complies with New York City's Salary Transparency Law for Job Advertisements. The salary applies specifically to the position being advertised and does not include benefits. Actual total compensation may vary based on factors such as experience, skills, qualifications, historical performance, and other relevant criteria.

description:
Job Summary

Towson University (TU) has earned distinction as both a top-100 public university and one of the nation's great colleges to work for. Located north of Baltimore, TU currently enrolls over 19,000 students and is the second-largest university in the prestigious University System of Maryland. Towson University fosters a climate that is grounded in respect to cultivating the intellectual and personal growth of the entire university community.

The Advancement Operations Coordinator supports the daily functions of Advancement Operations within the Division of University Advancement. This role is integral to ensuring the accuracy, integrity, and efficiency of advancement systems and processes, including database administration, maintenance of biographical and constituent records, and the timely and accurate processing of gifts. Telework may be available for this position up to two days per week following the completion of 90 days of service in good standing.

Responsibilities and Duties

Manage the biographical maintenance process and ensure the quality of data entry through standard quality control reporting and enforcement of established data entry procedures.

Coordinate with other advancement services colleagues regarding data entry assignments (i.e., administrative assistant and student worker); prioritize tasks and provide feedback as needed.

Oversee the generation and electronic distribution of weekly gift reports to account program directors for gift accounting and recognition purposes.

Assist with gift processing as needed. This involves preparing checks for deposit and preparing online gifts for processing; entering philanthropic gifts and pledges into the fundraising/donor database; and working with the TU Foundation employees to ensure all gift entries reconcile.

Collaborate with faculty and staff across campus who gather demographic information on alumni, parents, and students and determine best method for capturing and recording that information in the alumni database; this involves establishing protocols for the exchange of data and the prioritization of data entry tasks.

Qualifications and Skills

Bachelor’s degree acquired by 6/1/26

Two years of related experience.

Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel and SharePoint.

Demonstrated attention to detail while meeting obligatory deadlines.

Strong analytical, interpersonal, organizational, research and project management skills.

Ability to handle multiple assignments and changing priorities as circumstances may dictate.

Ability to work well both independently and as part of a team.

Maintain high level of professionalism and confidentiality.

Preferred qualifications:

Experience using fundraising software such as Ellucian Advance is preferred.

Preferred familiarity with gift accounting, IRS regulations, and CASE Management & Reporting Standards.

Salary and Benefits

Salary at $54,000 annually and full University benefits include 22 days of annual leave, up to 17 holidays, personal and sick days; excellent health, life, and retirement plans; and

This position will be open for a minimum of 14 days. A cover letter and resume with your online application is required.

Organization: University Advancement Division
Job Posting: Apr 16, 2026

description:
*Company: Leading Mortgage GSE*

*Location: McLean, VA*

*Duration: Contract to hire*

*Pay Rate: T.B.D, flexible*

*Our Impact:*
The Multifamily Technology Team’s mission is to be the preeminent Multifamily housing finance digital marketplace and establish an industry-leading platform in commercial real estate. Our vision is to modernize and sustain our technology to enable Freddie Mac Multifamily business to efficiently achieve our mission and deliver corporate and division objectives.
*Your Impact:*
The Product Analyst will be part of the Multifamily Technology team, supporting product delivery and business transformation for the Multifamily function for the Capital Markets business line. This role partners closely with Product Owner, delivery teams, and stakeholders to help clarify needs, translate them into well-defined requirements, and measure outcomes.
Your work supports decision-making and execution across three primary areas:
Customer & process understanding: Support Product Owner to partner with internal and external customers to understand needs and operational workflows and translate them into clear requirements and insights.
Strategy-to-execution support: Help connect business strategy to backlog refinement by providing analysis, sizing inputs, and outcome visibility.
Representation & partnership: Serve as a key contributor and subject matter resource for
Multifamily Technology, including coordination with connected teams.

*Qualifications*:

description:
At Vericel Corporation, we are pioneers in advanced cell therapies for sports medicine and severe burn care, transforming patient lives through cutting-edge regenerative medicine. Our commitment to innovation, patient-centered focus, and scientific excellence drives us to develop groundbreaking treatments including MACI (knee cartilage repair), Epicel and Nexobrid (burn care).

Position Summary:

As we continue to grow, we are looking for an People Operations Coordinator to be the heartbeat of our workplace. This is a high-visibility role where you’ll be the face of our office while gaining hands-on experience in People Operations and Talent Acquisition.

Whether you are a recent graduate looking to launch a career in HR or a professional with a few years of experience looking for a more dynamic, people-centric environment, this role offers a front-row seat to how a growing company operates.

Schedule: Onsite Monday through Friday in our new office in Burlington, MA.

Responsibilities:

HR & Talent Acquisition Support

Partner with our TA Manager in reviewing resumes, manage job postings, and maintaining our Applicant Tracking System (ATS) to keep our pipeline organized.
Lead the coordination and scheduling of interviews across multiple teams, managing complex calendars and logistics.
Provide administrative support for HR programs, reporting, and projects.
Help drive and execute internal programs and employee engagement initiatives that make our office a great place to work.
Support the HR inbox by ensuring timely responses and proper routing of employee and candidate inquiries.
Supporting employee lifecycle activities and logistics throughout onboarding, orientation and offboarding.

Workplace Experience & Office Operations

Lead front-of-office experience, setting standards for a professional, secure, and welcoming environment.
Collaborate with safety staff to guide visitors and ensure adherence to security protocols.
Own and optimize workplace operations, meeting logistics, and employee experience initiatives.
Ensure a clean, organized, and efficient office environment, including communal areas and digital displays.
Oversee kitchen inventory, shower room supplies, and wellness room supplies.

What we’re looking for:

0–3 years of experience in an administrative, coordination, or customer-facing role.
Strong organizational and time management skills with the ability to multitask effectively.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Maintains a business professional appearance and demeanor.
High attention to detail and a strong sense of ownership.
Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion.
Proactive, resourceful, and eager to learn and grow.
Interest in developing a career in Human Resources, Talent Acquisition, or People Operations.
Comfortable sitting for long time and being independent.
Ability to be onsite 5 days a week.

Why Vericel?

Cutting-Edge Science: Work with a leading regenerative medicine product that is transforming patient care.
Career Growth: Be a part of a growing organization with opportunities to expand your impact.
Collaborative Culture: Work alongside a team of dedicated professionals who are passionate about improving lives.

The salary range Vericel reasonably and in good faith expects to pay for this position at the time of this posting is $60,000 to $70,000 annually.

The actual salary offered will be determined based on factors such as the candidate’s qualifications, experience, and skills. Bonus, incentive pay, equity and benefits may be provided in addition to the base compensation listed above.

In accordance with Massachusetts law, Vericel provides the pay range that it reasonably and in good faith expects to pay for a particular and specific employment position at the time of posting or offer. This range is subject to change based on business needs, market conditions, and individual qualifications. Employees and applicants may request the pay range for their position or for a position to which they are applying. Retaliation for making such a request is strictly prohibited.

EEO Statement

All applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, or protected veteran status and will receive consideration for employment and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. Vericel Corporation is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer.

Vericel Corporation is VEVRAA federal contractor and desires priority referrals of protected veterans for job openings at all locations within the state.

description:
The *Strategic Account Analyst* plays a critical role in supporting the Strategic Accounts team by gathering, analyzing, and translating data into actionable insights and client-ready presentations. This role focuses on turning complex account data into clear, strategic information that supports decision-making, strengthens customer relationships, and drives growth within our largest accounts.

The analyst will work closely with the Strategic Accounts Manager and Major Accounts Sales team to identify trends, monitor account performance, prepare customer presentations, and support strategic planning initiatives.

This position requires a highly organized and analytical individual who thrives in a fast-paced environment, is comfortable working with large data sets, and can partner effectively with high-energy sales leaders.

This role also requires the ability to transform complex datasets into visually compelling dashboards, executive summaries, and strategic presentations that clearly communicate insights to both internal leadership and enterprise customers. The analyst should be comfortable using modern visualization and presentation tools such as Power BI, AI-assisted analytics tools, and design platforms to produce polished and engaging business materials. The ideal candidate is a proactive problem solver who is independently motivated to investigate data, identify issues, and develop solutions without needing constant direction. This role requires intellectual curiosity, critical thinking, and a strong desire to uncover insights that improve account performance and support strategic decision-making.

*Key Responsibilities*

*Strategic Account Data & Analysis*

* Gather, organize, and analyze data related to major accounts including revenue, product performance, service metrics, and growth opportunities.
* Identify trends, insights, and opportunities within key accounts to support strategic planning and account development.
* Maintain reporting dashboards and account performance tracking tools.
* Prepare account summaries, forecasting models, and performance analysis for leadership review.
* Develop and maintain data visualization dashboards (Power BI or similar platforms) that provide clear visibility into account performance, device utilization, service trends, and growth opportunities.

*Presentation & Sales Support*

* Build client-ready presentations, executive summaries, and account review materials for strategic account meetings.
* Translate complex data into clear visual insights and compelling narratives for customers and internal leadership.
* Assist the Strategic Accounts Manager in preparing materials for quarterly business reviews (QBRs), strategic planning sessions, and client presentations.
* Ensure accuracy and consistency of data used in all customer-facing materials.
* Design visually engaging presentations, proposals, and executive summaries using tools such as PowerPoint, Adobe, Canva or similar design platforms to elevate the quality and impact of strategic account communications.
* Translate analytical findings into clear visual storytelling that helps customers and leadership quickly understand trends, opportunities, and strategic recommendations.

*Strategic Account Coordination*

* Collaborate with sales, operations, finance, and service teams to collect and validate account data.
* Support the Strategic Accounts Manager in coordinating projects, initiatives, and reporting related to major accounts.
* Help ensure key account initiatives stay organized, on track, and supported with the appropriate data.
* Partner with internal teams to ensure strategic account insights are aligned with operational data, service metrics, and financial performance.

*Process Improvement*

* Identify opportunities to improve reporting processes, data accessibility, and presentation efficiency.
* Help implement tools or processes that enhance visibility into account performance.
* Explore opportunities to leverage automation, AI-assisted analytics, and improved visualization tools to increase reporting speed, accuracy, and strategic insight generation.

*Qualifications*

*Education & Experience*

* Bachelor’s degree in Business, Data Analytics, Marketing, or related field preferred.
* 2–5 years experience in sales analytics, business analysis, strategic account support, or similar role.
* Experience supporting enterprise or major accounts preferred.

*Skills & Competencies*

* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
* Advanced proficiency in Excel and data analysis tools.
* Experience developing dashboards and visual reports using Power BI, Tableau, or similar business intelligence tools preferred.
* Strong presentation development skills (PowerPoint or similar).
* Strong visual design instincts and the ability to produce polished, executive-level presentations and reports.
* Ability to translate data into meaningful insights and recommendations.
* Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines.
* Familiarity with modern productivity and design tools such as Adobe, Power BI, Excel, PowerPoint, Canva, AI-assisted analytics platforms, or presentation design tools is a plus.

*Portfolio Requirement*

Candidates may be asked to provide examples of dashboards, reports, or presentations they have developed.

Pay: $70,000.00 - $80,000.00 per year

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Professional development assistance
* Referral program
* Vision insurance



Application Question(s):
* What is your personal email address?

Education:
* Bachelor's (Preferred)

Experience:
* Sales analytics: 4 years (Preferred)
* Data analytics: 4 years (Preferred)
* Power BI: 4 years (Required)

License/Certification:
* Driver's License (Required)

Ability to Commute:
* Reading, PA 19611 (Required)

Work Location: In person

description:
Operations Coordinator

Be part of a transformative effort to improve the health and well-being of rural communities across Indiana.

Our client is leading Indiana’s 5-year Rural Health Transformation Program, including the Make Rural Indiana Healthy Again Regional Grants, a historic $600M investment to strengthen healthcare access, prevention, and system resilience across 64 fully rural and nine partially rural counties. This initiative brings together state leadership, healthcare providers, and community organizations to design and implement solutions that reflect local needs while advancing a shared, statewide vision for better health outcomes.

This role sits at the center of statewide program coordination, ensuring complex initiatives stay aligned, organized, and moving forward.

About the Role

The Operations Coordinator plays a critical role in supporting the statewide execution of the Rural Health Transformation Program (RHTP). Working closely with the Deputy Director of Operations, this position helps ensure that program activities remain coordinated, tracked, and aligned across multiple agencies, partners, and shared services teams.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in complex, fast-moving environments and enjoys bringing structure and clarity to large-scale initiatives. You will serve as a central point of coordination across financial, procurement, communications, and operational workstreams, helping leadership maintain visibility into progress, risks, and priorities.

By supporting implementation tracking, facilitating communication, and maintaining operational systems, you will help ensure that a major statewide investment delivers meaningful, measurable outcomes for rural communities.

What You’ll Do
Program Coordination & Implementation Support

Help drive alignment and execution across a complex, multi-partner statewide initiative.

Support monitoring of statewide program implementation activities and key milestones
Coordinate activities across program teams and shared services functions
Maintain awareness of program progress, risks, and operational needs
Help resolve cross-functional coordination challenges impacting delivery
Shared Services Coordination

Ensure critical support functions are aligned, responsive, and on track.

Coordinate engagement across finance, procurement, communications, legal, data, and technology teams
Track shared services requests, deliverables, and timelines
Support coordination meetings and follow-ups with internal service partners
Meeting & Task Management

Keep teams organized, accountable, and aligned on priorities.

Develop meeting agendas in collaboration with leadership
Capture and distribute meeting notes, decisions, and action items
Maintain task tracking systems and follow up on deliverables
Monitor deadlines and milestone completion
Financial & Procurement Tracking

Provide visibility into budget and procurement activity to support informed decision-making.

Maintain financial tracking tools for budgets and expenditures
Track procurement actions and maintain status logs
Identify potential delays or risks in financial or procurement processes
Reporting & Executive Support

Translate operational activity into clear, actionable insights for leadership.

Develop executive briefings, reports, and operational summaries
Prepare dashboards and status updates
Support materials for leadership meetings and stakeholder engagements
Stakeholder Communication & Coordination

Facilitate clear, consistent communication across a broad network of partners.

Coordinate communication among leadership, internal teams, and implementation partners
Support information flow between operational leadership and program teams
Draft internal updates and coordination communications
Administrative & Operational Support

Strengthen the systems and processes that keep the program running effectively.

Maintain program documentation and operational records
Support development of tracking tools and workflows
Contribute to process improvements that enhance coordination and efficiency
What You Bring
Skill Required / Desired Years of Experience
Bachelor’s degree in public administration, health administration, business administration, public health, or related field (or equivalent experience) Required
Experience supporting program operations, project coordination, or administrative operations in a complex organization Required 5 years
Experience coordinating across multiple stakeholders or functional teams Required
Experience with financial tracking or procurement processes Required
Experience preparing executive-level briefings or reports Required
Proficiency with project or task management tools and collaboration platforms Required
Experience supporting large-scale program implementation or statewide initiatives Highly Desired
Familiarity with public sector or healthcare program operations Highly Desired
Work Environment & Travel
Onsite role based at the Indiana Department of Health office in downtown Indianapolis
100% onsite work schedule
Standard schedule of 37.5 hours per week
Occasional travel may be required to support meetings or implementation coordination
Travel reimbursement provided in accordance with Indiana State Travel policy
Why This Role Matters

This role ensures that one of Indiana’s most significant healthcare transformation efforts operates with clarity, coordination, and accountability. By supporting the systems, communication, and execution behind the program, you help translate strategy into action and enable meaningful improvements in healthcare access and outcomes for rural communities across the state.

  • Develop executive briefings, reports, and operational summaries
  • Prepare dashboards and status updates
  • Support materials for leadership meetings and stakeholder engagements

Stakeholder Communication & Coordination

Facilitate clear, consistent communication across a broad network of partners.

  • Coordinate communication among leadership, internal teams, and implementation partners
  • Support information flow between operational leadership and program teams
  • Draft internal updates and coordination communications

Administrative & Operational Support

Strengthen the systems and processes that keep the program running effectively.

  • Maintain program documentation and operational records
  • Support development of tracking tools and workflows
  • Contribute to process improvements that enhance coordination and efficiency

What You Bring

Skill
Required / Desired
Years of Experience

Bachelor’s degree in public administration, health administration, business administration, public health, or related field (or equivalent experience)
Required

Experience supporting program operations, project coordination, or administrative operations in a complex organization
Required
5 years

Experience coordinating across multiple stakeholders or functional teams
Required

Experience with financial tracking or procurement processes
Required

Experience preparing executive-level briefings or reports
Required

Proficiency with project or task management tools and collaboration platforms
Required

Experience supporting large-scale program implementation or statewide initiatives
Highly Desired

Familiarity with public sector or healthcare program operations
Highly Desired

Work Environment & Travel

  • Onsite role based at the Indiana Department of Health office in downtown Indianapolis
  • 100% onsite work schedule
  • Standard schedule of 37.5 hours per week
  • Occasional travel may be required to support meetings or implementation coordination
  • Travel reimbursement provided in accordance with Indiana State Travel policy

Why This Role Matters

This role ensures that one of Indiana’s most significant healthcare transformation efforts operates with clarity, coordination, and accountability. By supporting the systems, communication, and execution behind the program, you help translate strategy into action and enable meaningful improvements in healthcare access and outcomes for rural communities across the state.

positionName: Office Manager and Executive Assistant to the President & CEO
description:
*Office Manager and Executive Assistant to the President & CEO*

Greater Rochester Chamber of Commerce is seeking a dedicated and organized individual to manage office operations and executive level administrative tasks for the President & CEO. The ideal candidate will possess strong administrative skills, exceptional organizational and communication abilities, experience with virtual meeting technology, and a team player willing to assist where needed. Must be comfortable working independently in a fast-paced environment and can prioritize duties throughout the day, adjusting to rapidly changing needs and priorities. This role is also essential in supporting staff, managers, and the leadership team, while enhancing productivity within the office environment.

Greater Rochester Chamber of Commerce is the region’s one-stop shop for business support, influence, and community connection. Rooted in advocacy, compliance, talent, and belonging, our organization combines an unmatched depth and breadth of business support with the agility and dedication to turn possibility into progress and accelerate opportunity and action. Here, you’re in good company.

*Essential Job Functions *include but are not limited to:

*Executive Assistant:*

* Arranges breakfast/lunch meetings (food, space, etc.), travel arrangements, and calendar for the President & CEO, accurately and efficiently.
* Coordinates member and local business tours and visits for the CEO
* Maintains organized files, both paper and electronic
* Opens and sorts mail on a daily basis and delivers.
* Able to work independently with minimal supervision as well as with a team.

*Board Meetings and Finance Committee m*

* Maintains up to date list of board and committee members as well as assistants.
* Sends out meeting notices and prepares agenda, reports, and related materials.
* Makes appropriate room and food arrangements for each meeting.
* Attends and takes accurate minutes and distributes in a timely manner afterwards.
* Initiates board member conflicts disclosure survey process annually

*Vendor/Supply Management:*

· Coordinates vendor orders and deliveries, and handles issues/problems with the appropriate vendor.

* Maintains adequate stock of office supplies, as well as, kitchen supplies including coffee and related supplies, water supply, etc.
* Stocks employee snacks and drinks while managing to a set quarterly budget. Able to research and recommend alternatives to save on cost.

*Reception*:

* Receives and screens phone calls and also manages office visitors through The Receptionist unit.
* Keeps lobby area neat and materials updated and stocked.

*Building and Facilities*

* Submits maintenance requests to building management for repairs, issues, etc.
* Manages chamber conference room rental process, invoicing, room setup, technology setup, coffee and access passes day of meeting. Able to research and recommend alternatives to save on cost.

*Miscellaneous*

* Assists with membership database adds/changes, event setup onsite, assist with IT tasks, assists different departments with administrative tasks and project work, and other duties as required.
* Helps coordinate employee activities, i.e., picnics, holiday events, etc. utilizing staff as needed

*Qualifications and Education Requirements*

* Minimum High School Diploma. Associate’s degree preferred.
* Minimum of 4-6 years in an office manager and/or executive administrative assistant role.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office suite including Outlook, Word, Excel and PowerPoint. Data entry experience a plus.
* Handles confidential information with discretion.
* Comfortable with use of technology such as for virtual meetings and phone systems.
* Excellent phone, written, and in-person communication skills and ability to interact professionally with diverse populations including high-ranking officials.
* Customer service-focused individual with the ability to function in a sometimes fast-paced environment.
* Strong time management and organizational skills with ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously and prioritize effectively, while maintaining high attention to detail and accuracy.
* Strong attention to detail, accuracy, grammar, and proofreading skills.
* Ability to work independently and also collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.
* Proficient in multi-tasking and available to work non-traditional hours on occasion at events.
* Ability to interact with others effectively and competently to foster positive relationships with staff, customers, vendors, the general public, executives, etc.
* Ability to lift up to 30lbs (office supplies).

*Additional Notes*

* This is a full-time, non-exempt hourly position scheduled to work *37.5* hours per week. May be asked to work early morning, evening, or occasional weekends as needed and typically with advance notice.
* Annualized pay range: $60,000-$75,000 commensurate with experience. This is not a guarantee of compensation or pay rate. The compensation in the final offer will be determined based on a variety of factors including experience.
* Greater Rochester Chamber offers competitive benefits including health, dental, vision, 401k, HSA/FSA, vacation and sick time, volunteer time, tuition reimbursement, and more.
* Expected to work in-office daily, with occasional flexibility for remote work.
* Our ideal candidate will embody our values of Inclusion, Passion, Integrity, Collaboration, and Service.

Greater Rochester Chamber of Commerce is an Equal Opportunity Employer. It is the policy of Greater Rochester Chamber to provide equal opportunity in employment and conditions of employment to all individuals regardless of age, race, color, religious beliefs, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, sex, veteran or military status, disability, pregnancy-related condition, predisposing genetic characteristics, genetic information, marital status, familial status, prior arrest, domestic violence victim status, non-job related convictions, participation in lawful activities outside of our workplace, or any other status protected by law.

Pay: $60,000.00 - $75,000.00 per year

Benefits:
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off

Work Location: In person

description:
Job Description Summary
Organization's Summary Statement:

Reporting to the Administrative Operations & Organizational Development, the Coordinator for Administrative Operations is a key operational support for Stamp. The position is embedded in the Administrative Operations team, which comprises various functions including, but not limited to, HR, payroll, strategic planning, training/development, and business/finance staff, and works on projects and initiatives related to these functional areas. This position works independently, under general guidance. It is not closely supervised and must be able to make decisions and use independent judgment.
The core duties of this role include:
-Conducting full oversight of the payroll, timekeeping, and onboarding logistics for all staff and new hires in Stamp (approximately 100 full-time and 30 graduate student staff and 400 undergraduate student staff) using Workday.
-Acting as the primary contact for onboarding affiliates and managing their status with Stamp vendors to resolve staffing or building-related issues.
-Serving as the primary HR Rep for Stamp for meetings with UHR and advising the Assistant and Associate Directors on operational needs based on staffing issues and pending HR changes.
-Troubleshooting Workday issues and accommodation needs for Stamp staff as they arise.
-Overseeing parking logistics (codes, assignments, department reps, etc.) for Stamp and Stamp Administrative Offices.
-Serving on departmental committees as related to an HR function or as requested.
-Act as a conduit of information/communication between Stamp and other campus units, such as the Office of the Vice President, departments of the Division of Student Affairs, and the Department of Transportation Services (DOTS); as well as update all internal and external websites and dashboards regularly.
-Supporting general administrative operations of Stamp.
-Provide administrative support to searches within Stamp, including communication, scheduling, job fair, and travel support.
-Hire, train, and supervise front desk Human Resource Assistant student employees and their work in the STAMP administrative offices
-Other duties as assigned.

Preferred Qualifications: Strong attention to detail; ability to connect systems and processes to understand the bigger picture; ability to work independently; Workday and/or HR experience; ability to learn and adapt to new technologies and systems.

Physical demands: Work takes place in both an office environment and in on-campus buildings; will require the ability to travel throughout campus. There may be occasions when the incumbent will need to work outside their normal shift to accommodate other shifts in the organization for meetings, training, etc.

Minimum Qualifications

Education: Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university.

Experience: One (1) year of professional secretarial, clerical, coordinator, or administrative experience.

Other: Additional work experience as defined above may be substituted on a year for year basis for up to four (4) years of the required education.

KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, & ABILITIES:

Knowledge of clerical and administrative procedures and systems such as filing and record-keeping.
Skill in oral and written communication.
Skill in the use of Microsoft Office products.
Ability to multitask and prioritize assignments.

Additional Job Details

Preferences:

Strong attention to detail; ability to connect systems and processes to understand the bigger picture; ability to work independently; Workday and/or HR experience; ability to learn and adapt to new technologies and systems.

Required Application Materials: Resume and cover letter

Posting Close Date: May 3, 2026

Job Risks
Not Applicable to This Position

Financial Disclosure Required
Yes

For more information on Financial Disclosure, please visit Maryland's State Ethics Commission website .

Department
VPSA-Stamp Union-Administration

Worker Sub-Type
Staff Regular

Salary Range
64,000-70,000

Benefits Summary

 .

Background Checks

Offers of employment are contingent on completion of a background check. Information reported by the background check will not automatically disqualify anyone from employment. Before any adverse decision, the finalist will have an opportunity to provide information to the University regarding disclosable background check information. The University reserves the right to rescind the offer of employment or otherwise decline or terminate employment if the information reported by the background check is deemed incompatible with the position, regardless of when the background check is completed.

Employment Eligibility

The successful candidate must complete employment eligibility verification (on Form I-9) by presenting documents that establish identity and work authorization within the timeframe required by federal immigration law, and where applicable, to demonstrate renewed employment authorization. Failure to complete employment eligibility verification or reverification within the timeframe set forth by law may result in suspension or termination of employment.

EEO Statement

The University of Maryland, College Park is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment. Please read the University’s Equal Employment Opportunity Statement of Policy.

description:
Join a dynamic engineering team where your organizational expertise and attention to detail will directly support innovative powertrain development. As an Administrative Assistant, you will play a key role in keeping operations running smoothly—partnering with engineers and leadership to streamline communication, coordinate activities, and ensure critical projects stay on track.

This is an opportunity to work in a fast-paced, collaborative environment with global exposure, where your contributions will have a visible impact on team success.

*Key Responsibilities*

* Manage calendars, schedule meetings, and coordinate travel for team members and leadership
* Prepare, format, and distribute technical reports, presentations, and meeting materials
* Track project updates, action items, and key deliverables to support team accountability
* Maintain organized records, documentation systems, and SharePoint sites
* Support onboarding, travel logistics, and day-to-day team operations
* Facilitate communication across cross-functional teams and global partners
* Assist with budget tracking, expense reporting, and administrative workflows

*Qualifications*

*Minimum Requirements*

* Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree, or equivalent professional experience
* 3+ years of recent experience providing administrative support to senior leadership
* Experience with SAP/Ariba
* Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word, Outlook, Teams)

Candidates must demonstrate advanced skills (80% or higher assessment score required)

* Excellent organizational and communication skills
* Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

*Preferred Qualifications*

* Experience supporting engineering or technical teams
* Experience working in a global environment, including coordination across time zones
* Familiarity with SharePoint or document management systems
* Exposure to data visualization or reporting tools (e.g., Power BI)
* Interest in leveraging new tools and technologies, including AI, to improve efficiency
* Experience supporting project tracking or reporting activities

*What You Bring*

* Strong communication and collaboration skills
* High level of organization and attention to detail
* Flexibility and adaptability in a changing environment
* A proactive mindset with the ability to anticipate team needs

\*\*\*1486682\*\*\*

*Company Overview*
Adapt Technology is a US-based engineering and staffing firm specializing in delivering innovative solutions across diverse industries such as automotive, aerospace, medical, and heavy equipment. We are committed to providing exceptional engineering services, staffing support, and technical expertise to help clients turn concepts into reality.

Pay: $22.00 - $27.00 per hour

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Employee discount
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Professional development assistance
* Referral program
* Vision insurance



Application Question(s):
* Will you now or in the future require VISA sponsorship?
* Describe how you prioritize and manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment while supporting leadership. What tools or methods do you use?
* Tell me about a time you supported a technical or cross-functional team. How did you ensure communication and deliverables stayed on track?

Education:
* High school or equivalent (Preferred)

Experience:
* administrative support for senior leadership: 3 years (Preferred)
* managing calendars and travel: 3 years (Preferred)
* sing SAP or Ariba: 2 years (Preferred)
* Excel and PowerPoint: 4 years (Preferred)
* handling reports and presentations: 3 years (Preferred)
* tracking projects or action items: 2 years (Preferred)
* maintaining SharePoint or records: 2 years (Preferred)
* working with global teams: 2 years (Preferred)
* managing budgets or expenses: 3 years (Preferred)

Ability to Commute:
* Ann Arbor, MI 48105 (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

description:
JobID: 690
Position Type:
 Office Staff/Operations Coordinator

Date Posted:
 4/15/2026

Location:
 Hiawatha College Prep - Northrop
(High-Needs School)

Hiawatha Academies is currently seeking a hard-working, passionate, and goal-oriented leader to join our team as a Operations Coordinator for the 2026-2027 school year.

Job Summary
The Operations Coordinator (OC) is responsible for implementing a powerful vision for supporting student achievement through helping to manage remote and site-based operations. The OC will support school leaders, operations staff, and classroom teachers by managing attendance systems, supporting food services and facilities, as well as day-to-day operations. We passionately believe that smooth and effective operations allow the instructional team to focus on preparing every student for success in college and in life. The Operations Coordinator is a member of the Operations Team and reports directly to the Dean of Operations (DO).

Why Hiawatha?
Hiawatha Academies is a network of college-preparatory schools focused on closing the opportunity gap in Minneapolis. We seek to create a K-12 school experience for scholars that empowers them with knowledge, character, and leadership skills. At Hiawatha Academies, we are committed to making our city and our world a more inclusive, equitable, and just place.
Our equity vision: to honor the humanity of all people, we actively disrupt systemic inequity in pursuit of an equitable world. Hiawatha Academies permanently disrupts educational inequity by ensuring a great school for every child.

Core Value and Beliefs
All Hiawatha Staff must demonstrate a passionate belief in and commitment to the following core values:
1) Children are at the center of everything we do.
2) Our mission requires bold thought and action.
3) We choose optimism.
4) We push ourselves to improve.
5) We are stronger together.
6) We create fun and a little weirdness.

Perks: Professional Development is scheduled into the calendar year and provides a time for all campuses to come together and celebrate our success.

Application Timeline: This Notice of Vacancy will be posted until filled.

Anticipated Start Date: July 13, 2026

Anti-Discrimination Policy and Commitment to Diversity
We believe that diversity is strength, and we are committed to maintaining an inclusive, multicultural network. We are an equal-opportunity employer and welcome all qualified applicants.

Attachment(s):
Operations Coordinator Job Description.docx.pdf

    Job Summary
    The Operations Coordinator (OC) is responsible for implementing a powerful vision for supporting student achievement through helping to manage remote and site-based operations. The OC will support school leaders, operations staff, and classroom teachers by managing attendance systems, supporting food services and facilities, as well as day-to-day operations. We passionately believe that smooth and effective operations allow the instructional team to focus on preparing every student for success in college and in life. The Operations Coordinator is a member of the Operations Team and reports directly to the Dean of Operations (DO).

    Why Hiawatha?
    Hiawatha Academies is a network of college-preparatory schools focused on closing the opportunity gap in Minneapolis. We seek to create a K-12 school experience for scholars that empowers them with knowledge, character, and leadership skills. At Hiawatha Academies, we are committed to making our city and our world a more inclusive, equitable, and just place.
    Our equity vision: to honor the humanity of all people, we actively disrupt systemic inequity in pursuit of an equitable world. Hiawatha Academies permanently disrupts educational inequity by ensuring a great school for every child.

    Core Value and Beliefs
    All Hiawatha Staff must demonstrate a passionate belief in and commitment to the following core values:
    1) Children are at the center of everything we do.
    2) Our mission requires bold thought and action.
    3) We choose optimism.
    4) We push ourselves to improve.
    5) We are stronger together.
    6) We create fun and a little weirdness.

    Perks: Professional Development is scheduled into the calendar year and provides a time for all campuses to come together and celebrate our success.

    Application Timeline: This Notice of Vacancy will be posted until filled.
    Anticipated Start Date: July 13, 2026

    Anti-Discrimination Policy and Commitment to Diversity
    We believe that diversity is strength, and we are committed to maintaining an inclusive, multicultural network. We are an equal-opportunity employer and welcome all qualified applicants.

    Attachment(s):

Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor's degree. Three years of administrative experience including personnel, financial administration, contracts administration, or procurement are required.
Recruitment Notes

POSITION SUMMARY

Working under the direction of the Facilities and Security Operations Director, this position requires a highly responsible professional with extensive experience to support administrative operations. This position will exercise considerable independent judgment and will function as the director’s high‑level administrative and project management support, leading the development and maintenance of policies and procedures while overseeing departmental strategic goals and measures. The position requires frequent interaction with the organization's leadership, department heads, employees, and external stakeholders, demanding exceptional organizational, analytical, and communication skills. These abilities are essential for preparing comprehensive reports, developing and managing the budget, reporting grant reimbursement reporting, maintaining operational dashboards, coordinating interdepartmental initiatives and other administrative tasks required to support operations.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

Experience working in property management, real estate, governmental, judicial, constitutional office, or large public sector environment.
Experience supporting facilities management, capital project administration, security operations, or public infrastructure programs.
Demonstrated experience in contracts, budget development, financial forecasting, grant administration, and reimbursement tracking within a public sector framework.
Familiarity with procurement processes, contract administration, and vendor oversight in accordance with governmental regulations.
Experience serving as liaison between operational divisions and central support functions such as Human Resources, Finance, OMB, or Procurement.
Experience drafting or revising policies, standard operating procedures, and internal controls.

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

Administers and manages all departmental HR/personnel activities as liaison to COCC Human Resources Department.
Assists with the development of the division's annual operating budget and provides revenue and expenditure analysis when requested by senior management, analyzing possible opportunities for improvement, recommending, and implementing solutions, as applicable.
Develops, proposes, revises, and implements departmental plans, policies, and procedures.
Assists the Director with the management and coordination of varied projects.
Performs other varied high-level administrative duties, as assigned.

KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES

Knowledge of project management principles and administrative practices.
Ability to independently identify, initiate, and implement process improvements, demonstrating strong self‑motivation and initiative.
Skill in maintaining effective professional relationships through proactive communication and collaborative engagement across divisions.

· Ability to show initiative in staying current with industry best practices and innovative approaches

Maintain flexibility to adjust priorities based on organizational needs.
Proficiency in Microsoft Word to create, format, and edit various documents such as reports, letters, and memos.
Expertise Microsoft Excel, demonstrating the ability to manage and analyze data, format spreadsheets, and generate charts and graphs.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite applications (Work, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.) including pivot tables, financial modeling, and data visualization tools.
Ability to communicate both verbally and in written formats to various levels within the organization.
Excellent analytical and problem- solving skills.
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.

WORK ENVIRONMENT AND PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS

Work is primarily performed in office environment, with extended periods at a computer. Occasional travel to COCC buildings and/or facilities may be required.

NOTE

This description is a representative summary of major job duties. Additional responsibilities may be assigned, as necessary.
