description:
The LMS Systems Operations Coordinator serves as the lead system administrator for UB Learns, the University at Buffalo’s learning management system (LMS) powered by D2L Brightspace. This position is responsible for ensuring that UB Learns functions with non-impactful interruption to faculty and students and managing third-party technology integrations into the LMS. This position collaborates daily with the UB Learns system management team, UB Continuing Professional Education (UB CPE) staff, and instructional design personnel to provide LMS end-user support to the university community. The LMS Systems Operations Coordinator works closely with university partners, including UB Information Technology, on system configuration and performance, support workflows and policies, and product review and evaluation. This position includes an 8–12 month period of shadowing, mentorship, and progressive responsibility under the guidance of the current Senior Programmer-Analyst.

Key accountabilities and responsibilities:

The position will receive training and mentorship from the UB Learns system administrator, with responsibilities progressively transferred over time, including:

Serve as UB’s lead LMS system administrator, including collaboration with vendor technologists and UB Enterprise Application Services (EAS) staff, to ensure uninterrupted and error-free LMS access and usage to faculty and students.
Manage integrations of third-party learning technologies into the LMS, including evaluating application needs and functions, addressing data safety and privacy concerns, and creating support policies.
Collaborate with university stakeholders to monitor and evaluate LMS configuration and support procedures, and update as needed, to ensure superior system performance and customer service.
Develop support policies and workflows and manage the creation and maintenance of support documentation and resources.
Collaborate with the CATT Instructional Support team to assist faculty in developing course content, designing assessments, and creating accessible instructional materials.

About the Department:
The Office of Curriculum, Assessment, and Teaching Transformation (CATT) at the University at Buffalo promotes educational excellence through: support of instructional and learning environments design; assessment of program and teaching effectiveness; and dissemination of evidence-based instructional strategies. Each team within the department supports the mission of educational excellence. Curriculum and Educational Effectiveness (CEE) supports program improvements through the program assessment and review process and through student evaluations of teaching. The Instructional Innovation team supports the effective design of courses and curricula through evidence-based approaches. The office of Learning Environments and Technologies works to establish in-person and virtual learning environments that promote effective teaching and learning. For more information, please visit the CATT website.

Learn more:

Our benefits, where we prioritize your well-being and success to enhance every aspect of your life.
Being a part of the University at Buffalo community.

University at Buffalo is an affirmative action equal opportunity employer and, in keeping with our commitment, welcomes all to apply including veterans and individuals with disabilities.

Minimum Qualifications
Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science or related area.
Three (3) years of experience administering, maintaining, and providing user support for a Learning Management System (LMS).

Preferred Qualifications
Strong preference for direct D2L Brightspace systems experience.
Experience in a higher education environment, particularly in supporting faculty and students with online learning needs.
Experience managing SaaS (Software as a Service) platforms and web-based applications, particularly handling user management and system configurations.
Experience with Customer Relationship Management (CRM) systems (e.g. TDX) for managing support requests and customer interactions.
Advanced written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to convey complex information effectively to technical professionals and non-technical end-users.

Physical Demands
Salary Range $90,000 - $95,000
Additional Salary Information
Job Type Full-Time
Campus Downtown Campus
Posting Alerts
Special Instructions Summary
Additional Information
Is a background check required for this posting? No
Background Check Notification

  • Three (3) years of experience administering, maintaining, and providing user support for a Learning Management System (LMS).

Preferred Qualifications
  • Strong preference for direct D2L Brightspace systems experience.
  • Experience in a higher education environment, particularly in supporting faculty and students with online learning needs.
  • Experience managing SaaS (Software as a Service) platforms and web-based applications, particularly handling user management and system configurations.
  • Experience with Customer Relationship Management (CRM) systems (e.g. TDX) for managing support requests and customer interactions.
  • Advanced written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to convey complex information effectively to technical professionals and non-technical end-users.

Physical Demands

Salary Range
$90,000 - $95,000

Additional Salary Information

Job Type
Full-Time

Campus
Downtown Campus

Is a background check required for this posting?
No

description:
*Job Overview*
We are seeking a dynamic and proactive Office/Human Resources Manager to lead our administrative operations and foster a positive, organized workplace environment. This role is essential in managing daily office functions, overseeing human resources activities, and ensuring smooth communication across teams. The ideal candidate will bring energy, excellent organizational skills, and a passion for creating an efficient and welcoming office atmosphere. You will be instrumental in coordinating events, suporting staff, managing vendor relationships, and maintaining comprehensive HR processes to support our company's growth and culture.

*Responsibilities*·

*.* Oversees general office projects and daily operations.

. Oversees all payroll, benefits, and employee related operations.

· Creates necessary data in the form of letters, memorandums, proposals, or reports, and composes correspondence for the owner's or manager’s signature.

· Maintains monthly insurance reports, and tracks and assists with insurance claims and paperwork.

· Maintains the company’s health and safety programs and assists with updating MSDS sheets and other documents as required.

· Provides support for the General Manager and other Executives as needed.

· Other duties as assigned.

*Skills*

Proven experience in office management, administrative support and human resources.

* Strong knowledge of human resources practices including hiring, onboarding, payroll (ADP a plus), and employee relations.
* Excellent organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple schedules and prioritize tasks effectively.
* Experience in event planning.
* Proficiency in using multi-line phone systems, calendar management tools, and basic accounting software.
* Effective communication skills with a focus on professionalism and clear interpersonal interactions.
* Ability to supervise teams, provide training & development opportunities, and foster a collaborative work environment
* Strong attention to detail in filing systems, record keeping, and compliance documentation.
* Join us in creating an energetic workplace where organization meets innovation! This role offers an exciting opportunity to lead vital operational functions while supporting our team’s growth and success.

Pay: $75,000.00 - $125,000.00 per year

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance



Experience:
* Human resources management: 2 years (Required)

Ability to Commute:
* Anchorage, AK 99515 (Required)

Work Location: In person

description:
At Columbia we come to work every day driven by the belief that innovation can transform industries, empower communities, and create a more efficient, sustainable world.

With operations spanning five continents and a commitment to excellence in service and support, our purpose extends beyond engineering advanced equipment. We are pioneering innovation and paving a better tomorrow with factory automation solutions worldwide.

*Summary:*

Provide essential administrative support for the Engineering & Service Solutions department (ESS) by implementing administrative systems, procedures and policies as well as administering engineering projects. Provides customer service support by assisting with service administrative processes and procedures. Scheduling outside service per communication with customers and field service technicians. Data entry of all associated time and travel expenses for service work into Dynamics CRM and Business Central. Supports CPSI Parts and Upgrades Sales Reps as well as CPSI Service Team with any travel arrangements, office administration or special projects (as needed).

*Essential Duties and Responsibilities:*

* *Assists CPSI with administrative duties.*
* Provides telephone support to Engineering Dept. when needed and requested
* Maintains customer files, scan customer’s information in Columbia’s database.
* Creates Project folders for ME and EE
* Maintains Engineering’s Daily Project Schedules (Mechanical, Electrical &Mold Engineering)
* Distributes month end reports to managers
* Collects mail and distributes
* Provides telephone support
* Manages the CPSI ECR-ECO process, as well as assists Engineering with the completion of requested changes.
* *Organizes and enters accurate data into Vault-tool (VT), BC (ERP), and Quote Manager (QM) databases*
* Processes Engineering equipment paperwork on all majors, daily sales orders, warranty orders with engineered items and distributes to the appropriate Mechanical and Electrical engineers
* Develops and enters item masters, part numbers, descriptions, product structures and other order information into the ERP.
* Records customer equipment data and order information into QM system.
* Reads as well as routes correspondence and inquiries appropriately
* Utilizes the Vault Tool to query existing drawings, part-numbers, etc. as well as checking out drawings and/or checking them in
* Utilizes ERP to view and approve Engineering time and make adjustments as needed.
* *Contributes to customer satisfaction by supporting internal and external customer service*
* Provides comprehensive customer assistance via phone or email
* Maintains a professional demeanor with customers and establishes a rapport to maintain overall customer satisfaction
* Customer file maintenance
* Utilizes Dynamics CRM to manage customer relationships; understand and documents internal and external customer needs and expectations
* Communicates to appropriate team(s) when an expectation will not be met
* *Administrative support for ESS staff*
* Processes initial service paperwork and distributes to the appropriate person
* Maintains the appointment schedule and travel arrangements for Field Service staff
* Maintenance of Service Call (D365) database
* Completes expense reports and credit card reconciliations for Engineering & Service staff
* Reviews service team utilization records and prepares service billings
* Process time sheets
* Process credit card statements
* *Support for the ESS Manager*
* Completing expense reports and credit card reconciliations
* Reviews department expense records
* Processes warranty orders
* *Assist Field Service Technicians with obtaining necessary visas and passports*
* *Contributes to team effort*
* Willing to attempt new assignments or fill in for others. Back up for Receptionist (as needed)
* Enters Item Masters for CP & Electrical Engineers, Production Control, Purchasing, Parts Sales/
* Supports Palletizer Engineering when needed
* Other duties as assigned
* *Non-Essential Job Functions*
* Back up support for Parts and Warranty

*Qualifications: *

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

*Knowledge, skills and/or abilities: *

_*Knowledge*_-

* LEAN Principles
* Software
* Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PPT, Outlook)
* D365
* Accounting Software (BC) _preferred_

_*Skills*_-

* Clerical Secretarial
* Written and Oral Communication
* People and phone skills for internal and external customer interactions
* Organizational
* Computer/Technical

_*Abilities-*_

* Ability to foster and work within a team environment
* Ability to work on multiple projects at one time
* Ability to work under pressure and time constraints
* Good reading, writing, and oral communication skills
* Displays attention to detail and consistently follows Standard Work Instructions (when available) when completing tasks
* Demonstrates the qualities of a "Team Player" by accepting new responsibilities and filling in for other employees when needed

*Education and/or Experience:*

* High School Diploma or GED _required_
* Associates degree or equivalent experience _preferred_
* 1-3+ years of work experience _preferred_
* Familiarity with job costing as it relates to billings

Columbia offers a full benefits package including medical, dental, vision, prescription drug, life insurance, flexible spending accounts, short and long term disability, 401(k), incentive compensation, paid holidays, paid time off, and tuition reimbursement. Equal Opportunity Employer - Women and Minorities are encouraged to apply. Columbia does participate in E-Verify. To apply for this exciting career opportunity today, please apply online at www.columbiamachine.com

Job Type: Full-time

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Employee assistance program
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Professional development assistance
* Referral program
* Tuition reimbursement
* Vision insurance

Schedule:

* 8 hour shift
* Day shift
* Monday to Friday

Ability to commute/relocate:

* Denver, CO: Reliably commute or planning to relocate before starting work (Required)

Work Location: In person

Pay: $46,000.00 - $58,000.00 per year

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Employee assistance program
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Referral program
* Vision insurance



Experience:
* Lean manufacturing: 1 year (Required)
* Schedule management: 1 year (Required)

Location:
* Denver, CO 80216 (Required)

Work Location: In person

description:
Group Presentation

With customers in over 100 countries served by more than 50,000 employees, TK Elevator achieved sales of around €9 billion in fiscal year 2022/2023. We ensure high levels of customer service globally from our extensive network of about 1,000 locations. Over the past several decades, TK Elevator has established itself as one of the world’s leading elevator companies and became independent after separation from the thyssenkrupp group in August 2020. The company’s most important business line is the service business proudly provided by around 25,000 service technicians. The product portfolio ranges from standardized elevators for low-rise residential buildings to highly customized solutions for skyscrapers. In addition, it covers escalators, moving walks, passenger boarding bridges and stair and platform lifts. Integrated cloud-based solutions, such as the MAX platform, are delivering enhanced services. With these digital offerings, there are no longer any limits to urban mobility. TKE – move beyond.

What we expect

0The first 3 letters in workplace safety are Y-O-U!

TK Elevator is currently seeking an experienced Operations Coordinator - New Installation/Modernization in Miami, FL.

Responsible for coordinating all administrative duties associated with the branch Modernization and New Installation operations departments so that the departments function efficiently and cost effectively.

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:

Sends turnover approval requests to turnover “mailbox” for all jobs that are scheduled for final inspection during the month. Keeps JobSight and inter-department calendars accurate.
Processes final acceptance forms from the field
Receives NI Maintenance audit reports; updates JobSight and sends to NI Maintenance Audit email.
Tracks jobs with NI Maintenance and/or interim maintenance and works closely with Sales Contract Administrator to ensure proper holds and/or adjustments are placed on existing service accounts under Modernization.
Receives completed booking packages from sales; reviews booking package for terms and conditions pertaining to certified payroll, New Installation Maintenance warranty and any other pertinent terms.
Prepares project file, truck folder, mechanic folder and Letter One Package using the booking packages provided by sales.
Reviews distributor report for ship dates and updates JobSight. Includes reviewing the file to determine appropriate permits needed, preparing the permits, and preparing the intent to install forms to obtain the permit.
Prepares and logs change orders into JobSight. Includes following up on outstanding change orders, booking change orders, and providing time tickets/documentation to the customer as needed.
Prepares documentation and attends the weekly operations meetings and monthly order management calls. Includes assigning tasks during/after the meeting. If a project requires onsite visits the coordinator may be asked to attend the kick-off meeting.
Completes all project closeout documentation and sends to required recipients.
Fields calls from customers regarding status of jobs and answers inquiries.
Participates in the monthly Account Receivable conference call with Regional Collections. Actively pursues and follows-up on Account Receivable items. Tracks and sends deposit checks to Regional Collections.
Assists in the preparation of payroll in JobSight for Superintendent approval.
Receives and reviews union vacation request forms for conflicts and available time. Tracks time-off requests utilizing Vacation Tracker in our systems, forwards reports to designated parties.
Submits warranty claims and accurately tracks to ensure timely processing of the warranty.
Creates parts requisitions based on accurate and detailed documentation from field and/or operations management. This may include safety, uniforms, and supplies.
Maintains and analyses various daily, weekly and monthly reports. Includes resolving open commitments, expected receipts, and jobs eligible to close reports.

Who we are looking for

EDUCATION & EXPERIENCE:

High school diploma or GED (general education degree); or one-year certificate from college or technical school
Six months related experience and/or training in basic business administration; or an equivalent combination of education and experience
Previous elevator repair administrative work
Systems database knowledge
Ability to utilize a laptop, desktop computer, or tablet, cell phone if needed

Contact

To apply to a position, please click on the Apply Now button.

For any additional questions or job specific requests, please use the contact
below and include the Job Requisition Number as a reference.

Elevatorjobs.AMS@tkelevator.com

What we offer

Provided they meet all eligibility requirement under the applicable plan documents, employees will be offered

Medical, dental, and vision coverage
Flexible spending accounts (FSA)
Health savings account (HSA)
Supplemental medical plans
Company-paid short- and long-term disability insurance
Company-paid basic life insurance and AD&D
Optional life and AD&D coverage
Optional spouse and dependent life insurance
Identity theft monitoring
Pet insurance
Company-paid Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Tuition reimbursement
401(k) Retirement Savings Plan with company match: Employees can contribute a portion of their pay on a pre-tax or Roth basis. The company provides a dollar-for-dollar match on the first 5% contributed.

Additional benefits include:

15 days of vacation per year
11 paid holidays each calendar year (10 fixed, 1 floating)
Paid sick leave, per company policy
Up to six weeks of paid parental leave (available after successful completion of 90 days of full-time employment)

Eligibility requirements for these benefits will be controlled by applicable plan documents. This is intended to provide a general description of benefits and other compensation and is not a substitute for applicable plan documents or company policies.

Additional information

TK Elevator is an equal opportunity employer and committed to diversity. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, gender, race, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, and veteran status or any other protected status required by applicable law.

Applicants with disabilities who require reasonable accommodation in connection with the application process are encouraged to email us directly at ElevatorJobs.ams@tkelevator.com or call 1-844-427-5461.

description:
What we expect
The first 3 letters in workplace safety are Y-O-U!TK Elevator is currently seeking an experienced Operations Coordinator- Service/Repair in Manhattan, NY.

Responsible for performing all administrative duties associated with the service operations department so that the department functions efficiently and cost effectively.

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:

Performs Install Base maintenance as needed. Creates safety inspection tickets.
Updates on-call lists, sends detailed notification to Dispatch daily. Checks technicians in/out, sends down car listing and communicates information to Dispatch and supervisors.
Provides status to National Accounts and customers on open work orders and completed callbacks. Updates evening ticket with the monthly work order number.
Acts as liaison between the branch operations and regional dispatch; maintains shared calendars for dispatch with field attendance and work scheduling. Reviews open ticket reports and submits to dispatch.
Tracks Operations systems and tool audits.
Assists mechanics with information technology downloads, on mobile devices and info into other Systems, ordering brochures and tools. Orders new phones and replacements for the service department. (N/A if branch has office manager.)
Assists managers with safety meetings and maintains documentation. (N/A if branch has office manager)
Completes manual payroll entry forms, includes daily DVR processing and cost corrections.
Creates parts requisitions and receives parts. Reviews weekly report of un-received invoices.
Performs research and review for Service Manager which may include running Account History reports, weekly pre-invoicing reports, high profile service account reports, Work in Process, Routing, TK Exact, missed service reports, sick unit reports, researching billable calls.
Sends copy of down payment checks to regional Accounts Receivable. Submits checks sent to branch for service to lockbox
Creates safety inspection tickets. Monitors pre-invoicing report to ensure Safety Inspections are not being processed as Preventative Maintenance Tickets/Manual tickets.
Assigns tickets to mechanics, as needed.
Compiles data/information for legal for First Report of Incidents and/or lawsuits (copies of contracts, tickets, and requested data) (assist office manager and/or contract sales admin to compile the required documentation).
Prepares badging applications and tracking; orders uniforms for service (if not ordered by warehouse).
Prepares special reports for high profile customers and provides National Accounts with updates and Requests for Information.
Reviews Work in Progress report (weekly) and submits to regional billers.
Reviews invoice on-hold reports and works with the Regional Procurement Department to correct.
Opens and distributes mail and faxes. (N/A if office has office manager)
Codes local Accounts Payable invoices and forward to Oracle Invoice email. (N/A if branch has office manager)
Manages vehicles and submits change forms to LeasePlan. (N/A if branch has office manager or warehouse supervisor)
Who we are looking for

EDUCATION & EXPERIENCE:

High school diploma or GED (general education degree); or one-year certificate from college or technical school; or three to six months related experience and/or training in basic business administration
Some elevator repair administrative work preferred
Oracle database knowledge

What we offer

Salary range $58,000- $75,400. The role offers an annual incentive program.

Provided they meet all eligibility requirement under the applicable plan documents, employees will be offered

Medical, dental, and vision coverage
Flexible spending accounts (FSA)
Health savings account (HSA)
Supplemental medical plans
Company-paid short- and long-term disability insurance
Company-paid basic life insurance and AD&D
Optional life and AD&D coverage
Optional spouse and dependent life insurance
Identity theft monitoring
Pet insurance
Company-paid Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Tuition reimbursement
401(k) Retirement Savings Plan with company match: Employees can contribute a portion of their pay on a pre-tax or Roth basis. The company provides a dollar-for-dollar match on the first 5% contributed.

Additional benefits include:

15 days of vacation per year
11 paid holidays each calendar year (10 fixed, 1 floating)
Paid sick leave, per company policy

descriptionHTML:
What we expect

The first 3 letters in workplace safety are Y-O-U!TK Elevator is currently seeking an experienced Operations Coordinator- Service/Repair in Manhattan, NY.

Responsible for performing all administrative duties associated with the service operations department so that the department functions efficiently and cost effectively.

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:

  • Performs Install Base maintenance as needed. Creates safety inspection tickets.
  • Updates on-call lists, sends detailed notification to Dispatch daily. Checks technicians in/out, sends down car listing and communicates information to Dispatch and supervisors.
  • Provides status to National Accounts and customers on open work orders and completed callbacks. Updates evening ticket with the monthly work order number.
  • Acts as liaison between the branch operations and regional dispatch; maintains shared calendars for dispatch with field attendance and work scheduling. Reviews open ticket reports and submits to dispatch.
  • Tracks Operations systems and tool audits.
  • Assists mechanics with information technology downloads, on mobile devices and info into other Systems, ordering brochures and tools. Orders new phones and replacements for the service department. (N/A if branch has office manager.)
  • Assists managers with safety meetings and maintains documentation. (N/A if branch has office manager)
  • Completes manual payroll entry forms, includes daily DVR processing and cost corrections.
  • Creates parts requisitions and receives parts. Reviews weekly report of un-received invoices.
  • Performs research and review for Service Manager which may include running Account History reports, weekly pre-invoicing reports, high profile service account reports, Work in Process, Routing, TK Exact, missed service reports, sick unit reports, researching billable calls.
  • Sends copy of down payment checks to regional Accounts Receivable. Submits checks sent to branch for service to lockbox
  • Creates safety inspection tickets. Monitors pre-invoicing report to ensure Safety Inspections are not being processed as Preventative Maintenance Tickets/Manual tickets.
  • Assigns tickets to mechanics, as needed.
  • Compiles data/information for legal for First Report of Incidents and/or lawsuits (copies of contracts, tickets, and requested data) (assist office manager and/or contract sales admin to compile the required documentation).
  • Prepares badging applications and tracking; orders uniforms for service (if not ordered by warehouse).
  • Prepares special reports for high profile customers and provides National Accounts with updates and Requests for Information.
  • Reviews Work in Progress report (weekly) and submits to regional billers.
  • Reviews invoice on-hold reports and works with the Regional Procurement Department to correct.
  • Opens and distributes mail and faxes. (N/A if office has office manager)
  • Codes local Accounts Payable invoices and forward to Oracle Invoice email. (N/A if branch has office manager)
  • Manages vehicles and submits change forms to LeasePlan. (N/A if branch has office manager or warehouse supervisor)

description:
DESCRIPTION

Our mission is to be Earth's most customer-centric company. This is what unites Amazonians across teams and geographies as we are all striving to delight our customers and make their lives easier, one innovative product, service, and idea at a time.

The Workforce Staffing (WFS) Team is a skilled, customer obsessed, business partner hiring across multiple locations for entry-level warehouse and fulfillment associate roles. We are looking for exceptionally talented, bright and driven people to help build the future Amazon talent pipeline.

The Workforce Staffing (WFS) Team is looking for an ambitious and enthusiastic leader that can inspire others to reach the highest level of service in every candidate interaction. As a leader, the Staffing Manager owns candidate generation, candidate experience and operational excellence for a defined geography (node). The Staffing Manager ensures delivery of business goals by creating a collaborative team environment with a strong candidate-oriented culture.

Staffing Managers direct a high-performing team of Staffing Coordinators, Administrators, and seasonal/flexible Associates who thrive in an innovative, fast-paced environment. These teams work onsite with varying schedules and require travel to multiple sites according to business needs. At Amazon, our teams roll up our sleeves, work hard, have fun and make history!

Key job responsibilities
• Manages the day-to-day operations to ensure a positive candidate experience and alignment with business goals
• Leads and develops a team of associates, including regular performance evaluations and coaching for performance improvement
• Assists with interviewing and onboarding new associates
• Coordinates team schedule and assigns duties based on business needs
• Understands new hire workflow and technical systems and makes recommendations to drive improvement
• Develops and maintains relationships with both internal and external stakeholders to build successful long-term partnerships
• Analyzes daily, weekly, and monthly reporting of Key Performance Indicators and provides meaningful solutions
• Leads and drives change initiatives and projects, of increasing scale and complexity, through to completion
• Forecasts labor demand plans and creates scalable staffing strategies to build infrastructure and ensure team resource capabilities meet business demand

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

- 2+ years of HR experience
- Bachelor's degree, or 2+ years of Amazon (blue badge/FTE) experience
- Knowledge of Microsoft Office products and applications (especially Excel) at an advanced level
- 2+ years of leading a team with experience in hiring and developing talent experience

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- 5+ years of HR Generalist or HR Specialist in a corporate environment experience
- 4+ years of using case management system, reviewing cases, determining appropriate processes and policies that apply to a specific situation, and resolving a case or establishing/executing a case management plan experience
- Experience developing and implementing of standards, policies and programs or equivalent
- Experience handling confidential information and maintaining professionalism in dealing with senior executives, or experience with end-to-end project management

Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

The base salary range for this position is listed below. Your Amazon package will include sign-on payments and restricted stock units (RSUs). Final compensation will be determined based on factors including experience, qualifications, and location. Amazon also offers comprehensive benefits including health insurance (medical, dental, vision, prescription, Basic Life & AD&D insurance and option for Supplemental life plans, EAP, Mental Health Support, Medical Advice Line, Flexible Spending Accounts, Adoption and Surrogacy Reimbursement coverage), 401(k) matching, paid time off, and parental leave. Learn more about our benefits at https://amazon.jobs/en/benefits.

USA, DE, New Castle - 85,600.00 - 149,800.00 USD annually

positionName: Anticipated**Coordinator of Athletics & Athletic Office Operations
description:
Anticipated**Coordinator of Athletics & Athletic Office Operations

Reports To: Athletic Director
Department: Athletics
Location: KHS/MSK
Position Type: Non-Union Exempt

Position Summary

The Coordinator of Athletics & Athletic Office Operations assists the Athletic Director in ensuring the efficient, compliant, and student-centered operation of the RSU 21 Athletic Department. This position supports daily athletic office functions, coordinates athletic programs and events for Kennebunk High School (KHS) and Middle School of the Kennebunks (MSK), manages eligibility and records, assists with financial and personnel operations, and serves as a primary point of contact for students, families, staff, officials, and community members. The Coordinator promotes the mission of RSU 21 by fostering educational athletics that emphasize safety, sportsmanship, inclusion, and student growth.

Job Goals
Support the mission, vision, and core values of RSU 21.
Provide equitable opportunities for all students to participate in interscholastic athletics.
Ensure safe, organized, and efficient athletic operations.
Promote positive relationships with students, families, staff, and the community.
Qualifications
Required / Preferred
Associate’s degree with a minimum of three (3) years of administrative assistant experience, or equivalent combination of education and experience.
Eligibility for State of Maine criminal history record check.
State of Maine Department of Education Athletic Director Endorsement (preferred or ability to obtain).
Registered Athletic Administrator (NIAAA) certification (preferred or ability to obtain).
Key Competencies
Highly organized with strong attention to detail.
Effective communicator and collaborator.
Strong customer-service orientation.
Calm and solution-focused in high-pressure situations.
Commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion.
Maintains knowledge of all computer programs used by the department and RSU 21.
Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities
Knowledge of interscholastic athletic programs and Maine Principals’ Association (MPA) regulations.
Strong organizational, communication, and customer-service skills.
Ability to manage confidential information with discretion.
Proficiency with office technology, accounting systems, databases, and athletic management platforms.
Ability to multitask, prioritize independently, and work effectively under pressure.
Essential Duties and Responsibilities
Athletic Program & Event Operations
Assist with daily operations of the Athletic Department.
Coordinate middle school athletic programs.
Coordinate schedules, transportation, facilities, and game-day logistics for KHS and MSK.
Schedule transportation using SchoolDude (Trip Direct).
Supervise game workers, ticketing, and event staff.
Verify ticket sales and ensure proper accounting procedures.
Coordinate awards nights, sports photos, and recognition events.
Complete certificates, letters, and pins; order plaques and awards.
Eligibility, Compliance & Records
Manage student-athlete eligibility in accordance with RSU 21 and MPA regulations.
Maintain participation records, rosters, schedules, and reports.
Manage MPA entries and tournament information.
Maintain up-to-date files for each coach.
Administrative & Office Management
Serve as receptionist for the Athletic Office.
Manage calls, emails, visitors, calendars, and correspondence.
Supervise interns and student workers.
Maintain copiers, printers, and office supplies.
Assist with emergency drills and attendance verification.
Financial Responsibilities
Assist with budget oversight with the RSU 21 Finance Office.
Process purchase orders, invoices, deposits, and credit card reconciliations.
Maintain stipend spreadsheets for KHS and MSK athletics.
Work with Human Resources regarding contracts, stipends, and payroll.
Assist with booster and activity accounts.
Equipment, Inventory & Vendor Management
Maintain uniform and equipment inventory.
Manage Helmet Tracker and asset records.
Establish vendor accounts and maintain relationships.
Maintain Master Library and athletic asset records.
Communication & Community Relations
Maintain athletic webpages and digital platforms.
Utilize Gipper and related tools to promote athletics.
Serve as liaison to booster organizations and parent groups.
Promote school spirit and sportsmanship.
Student-Centered Responsibilities
Assist students and families with athletic participation questions.
Maintain student records as appropriate.
Promote a safe, inclusive, and respectful athletic environment.
All Other Duties as Assigned

This job description describes the general nature and level of work performed. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of duties. RSU 21 reserves the right to modify this description at any time to meet the changing needs of the district’s athletic programs.

Systems & Software Utilized
Aptafund
ADP
School Dude (Trip Direct)
Gipper
KissFlow
Helmet Tracker
Rank One
Master Library
ArbiterSports / rSchoolToday
Additional Information
Reasonable Accommodations

RSU 21 will provide accommodations to an individual with a disability, as defined by the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) or applicable law, who has made the district aware of their disability, unless doing so would cause an undue hardship to the district.

Physical Demands

Employee is regularly required to sit, stand, walk, speak, and hear; may occasionally be required to lift or move up to 50 pounds.

Work Environment

Work environment is typically an office and athletic facility setting; evening, weekend, and occasional travel are required.

Terms of Employment

Year-round, full-time, non-union position; salary will be commensurate with the candidate’s qualifications and experience.

Evaluation

Performance will be evaluated by the Athletic Director in accordance with RSU 21 evaluation procedures.

Application Deadline

Position will remain open until a suitable candidate is found.

positionName: Law firm looking for bilingual (English & Chinese) receptionist/ Administrative Coordinator
description:
Reid & Wise LLC – Receptionist / Administrative Coordinator (Bilingual: English & Mandarin)

Location: One Penn Plaza, New York, NY

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $24.00 – $33.00 per hour (approx. $50,000 – $60,000 annually)

Position Overview

Reid & Wise LLC is seeking a bilingual (English & Mandarin) Receptionist / Administrative Coordinator for our Midtown Manhattan office. This role is primarily responsible for front desk operations, billing support, office management, and administrative coordination.

The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, highly organized, and able to manage multiple responsibilities in a fast-paced professional environment. While exposure to legal work is a plus, this is not a paralegal-focused position.

About Reid & Wise LLC

Reid & Wise LLC is an international law firm representing individual and business clients in complex commercial transactions, litigation, and business immigration matters. We have offices in New York, Los Angeles, and Shanghai. Our New York headquarters is located in a Class A office building in Midtown Manhattan. We pride ourselves on maintaining a collaborative and professional working environment.

Key Responsibilities

Front Desk & Client Coordination

Answer, screen, and direct incoming phone calls in a professional manner

Greet clients and visitors; manage reception area

Coordinate meeting schedules and conference room usage

Administrative & Office Management

Oversee daily office operations and general administrative functions

Manage office supplies, vendor coordination, and facility-related matters

Assist with internal coordination among attorneys, staff, and external parties

Billing & Financial Support

Assist with invoicing, billing records, and payment tracking

Support basic bookkeeping tasks (QuickBooks experience is a plus)

Coordinate with accounting personnel as needed

Website & Systems Maintenance

Assist with basic website updates and content management

Maintain internal databases and document systems

Additional Support

Provide general administrative supp

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $24.00 - $33.00 per hour

Benefits:
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Vision insurance



Language:
* Chinese (Required)

Work Location: In person

positionName: Workforce Scheduling Coordinator – Home Care Staffing Operations (Email to Apply!)
description:
Job Title: *Staffing Supervisor*
Compensation: *$25 – $28 DOE*
Benefits: *Full Benefits Package*
Location: *Golden Valley, MN*
Schedule: Full*-Time | Monday - Friday, 8:00am–5:00pm*

Successful applicants will email *Elena Ehrlich at elena.ehrlich@hhcare.net* with a summary of their healthcare staffing experience.

*About Home Health Care, Inc.:*
For over *30 years*, Home Health Care, Inc. has provided high-quality nursing, therapy, and caregiver services across 21 Minnesota counties. As a locally owned, Medicare-certified home health agency, we are committed to delivering exceptional care wherever our clients call home.

*Why You Will Love This Job:*

* Employer contributions toward medical insurance
* Employer-paid dental, vision, disability, and life insurance
* Supportive supervisors and strong team culture
* Up to *3 weeks PTO* + *6 paid holidays*
* Mission-driven, people-centered work every day

*Position Summary:*

We’re seeking a highly organized, compassionate *Staffing Supervisor* to ensure smooth and efficient care delivery. You’ll coordinate schedules, support field staff, resolve issues quickly, and build positive relationships with clients and caregivers. This role requires strong communication, multitasking, and a commitment to quality care.

*Key Responsibilities:*

*Schedule Coordination:*

* Create and manage client and field staff schedules
* Coordinate care needs with families and clinical teams
* Handle changes, last-minute needs, and emergencies efficiently

*Field Staff Support:*

* Maintain staff records, certifications, and availability
* Provide ongoing support and foster a positive team environment

*Client Relations:*

* Build strong, empathetic relationships with clients and families
* Serve as the primary point of contact for concerns and service requests
* Conduct periodic check-ins to ensure satisfaction

*Documentation & Reporting:*

* Maintain accurate records of schedules, staff info, and changes
* Prepare reports on availability, hours worked, and client satisfaction

*Quality Assurance:*

* Ensure care delivery meets agency standards
* Participate in initiatives to improve processes and service quality

*Team Collaboration:*

* Work closely with Care Managers, Nursing, HR, and admin teams
* Communicate clearly to support seamless care transitions

*Qualifications:*

* High school diploma required; Bachelor’s degree preferred

positionName: Logistics Coordinator (Korean Bilingual) – Forwarding, Inland Operations, Austin, Texas
description:
*Company Overview*

DSL USA, Inc. is seeking a qualified Logistics Coordinator / Operations Coordinator to support export and import forwarding operations.

*Location* : Austin, TX

*Key Responsibilities*

* Manage export/import forwarding operations including ocean, air, and inland transportation
* Coordinate shipment schedules, bookings, cargo tracking, and issue resolution
* Prepare and review export/import documentation such as Commercial Invoice, Packing List, B/L, and SLI
* Communicate and collaborate with carriers, trucking companies, customs brokers, warehouses, and overseas agents
* Maintain and update shipment data including freight rates, billing, and tracking records
* Support operational and inland transportation process improvements (lead time reduction, cost efficiency)
* Coordinate customer shipment schedules and assist with operational reporting and data management

*Qualifications*

* 1–2 years of experience in logistics, freight forwarding, trade, or operations preferred
* Entry-level candidates with relevant internship or practical experience are welcome to apply
* Korean and English bilingual required
* Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and basic office software required

*Employment Terms*

* Employment Type: Full-time
* Compensation: Negotiable based on experience
* Work Schedule: Monday – Friday

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $50,000.00 - $70,000.00 per year

Benefits:
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

positionName: Sales & Client Operations Associate (Fast-paced Profitable Fintech, Move to Monterey, CA!)
description:
*Sales & Client Operations Associate (Fast-paced Profitable Fintech, Move to Monterey, CA!)*

*Contract-to-Permanent | In-Office Only*

*Downtown Monterey, CA*

*Remote applicants will not be considered*

*About Us*

We are a leading *equity management and business valuation firm* serving private companies and venture-backed startups. Our platform helps founders, CFOs, and investors manage cap tables, stock options, and perform defensible, audit-ready valuations. We are a profitable Fintech company, and we would love to have you here in beautiful Monterey. We do things differently from the traditional VC-dependent companies.

In just a few years, we’ve grown to serve *25,000+ private companies*, with *$300B+ in assets under administration* and *$100B+ in valuations performed*, adding over *$5B in new valuations monthly*. We are revenue-generating, profitable, and scaling fast.

The firm is led by *seasoned fintech entrepreneurs* with a track record of building #1 category leaders and generating billions in revenue. You’ll work closely with senior leadership, sales, and product-this is not a “back-office” role.

Our culture is intense, collaborative, and execution-driven. We expect ownership, accountability, and excellence-but we reward it with real responsibility, accelerated growth, and long-term upside.

*About the Role*

As a *Sales & Client Operations Associate*, you will support the sales and revenue team by ensuring deals, client onboarding, and internal processes run efficiently. You will play a key role in coordinating onboarding activities, supporting the sales pipeline, and helping new clients successfully get started with Eqvista’s platform.

You will also provide cap table support to clients by assisting with *cap table setup, share issuances, and equity transfers*, ensuring clients can confidently use the platform and manage their equity data.

This is a *contract-to-permanent opportunity* designed for someone who wants to prove themselves quickly and grow into a long-term sales or revenue role. You will be *in the office five days a week* in downtown Monterey, working side-by-side with leadership and the sales team.

We believe *great people do their best work when they have trust, speed, and room to experiment.* If you want to try a new tool, test a better process, or move on a strong idea, *we’ll support you and help you execute fast.*

If you want remote flexibility or a low-intensity environment, this is not the role for you. If you want to *learn fast, contribute immediately, and build a serious career in fintech and professional services*, keep reading.

*In This Role, You Will*

* Coordinate client onboarding and implementation for new Eqvista customers.
* Assist clients with cap table demos and cap table support.
* Support the sales pipeline by assisting with outreach projects.
* Provide responsive platform support to clients and answer questions related to cap table functionality.
* Maintain accurate records of client activity, onboarding progress, and communications.
* Collaborate cross-functionally with product, valuation, and support teams to resolve client issues quickly.

*What you’ll need:*

* Educational _*or*_ work experience in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, Management, or a related field.
* Demonstrated ability to communicate complex concepts clearly and influence executive-level stakeholders.
* Highly detail-oriented with excellent organizational skills.
* Fast learner who can quickly grasp new software and equity concepts.
* Self-motivated, able to work independently, and responsible for managing deadlines and deliverables.
* Competitive, motivated, and committed to exceeding client expectations.
* Ability to quickly learn new software tools and concepts related to equity management and financial valuations.
* High integrity and professional demeanor centered on exceptional customer service.

*Non-Negotiables*

* Willingness to work in-office, five days per week, in Monterey, CA.
* Strong sense of responsibility and follow-through.
* Professionalism rooted in integrity and quality client service.

*Compensation & Growth*

This is a contract-to-permanent role, with clear performance benchmarks for conversion to full-time.

* Competitive hourly or contract rate (based on experience)
* Performance incentives and ESOP after 3 months

Pay: $45,000.00 - $55,000.00 per year

Work Location: In person

positionName: Operations & Maintenance Regional Coordinator - Jerome, ID
description:
Operations and Maintenance Regional Coordinator

Regenis

Regenis is on a mission to reimagine reusable resources as we build and operate anaerobic digesters on dairy farms. If you want to help family farms thrive while leaving a legacy of a cleaner planet, we’re looking to build a team of hard-working, high integrity teammates interested in meaningful careers that give you the room to grow your skills while supporting your family. Whether performing new construction on a digester or supporting the operations and maintenance at a well-established facility, Regenis sets our employees up for success through initial and ongoing training opportunities.

Compensation

Regenis is looking for a talented Operations and Maintenance Regional Coordinator to join the team full-time. This position pays $26 — $31 per hour.

Benefits

Regenis provides a comprehensive benefits package, including employer-paid medical, dental, and life insurance. Additional offerings include vision coverage, short- and long-term disability insurance, as well as hospital indemnity, critical illness, and accident insurance.

In addition, Regenis team members enjoy a 401(k) plan with company match, referral bonuses, paid holidays, and generous PTO accrual. We support growth and well-being through education reimbursement, ongoing training, professional development and certification opportunities, and access to an Employee Assistance Program and an on-staff counselor.

Job Description

Key responsibilities:

Logistics & Vendor Coordination

Coordinate and dispatch vendors to job sites based on operational needs
Schedule and track vendor services to ensure timely completion
Review and approve vendor invoices for accuracy and completeness

Inventory & Parts Management

Manage inventory levels, including tracking, organizing, and restocking materials and parts
Order parts and supplies to ensure cost-effective and timely availability
Coordinate and perform delivery of parts to job sites and internal teams

Fleet, Tools & Equipment Management

Oversee vehicle maintenance schedules to ensure safety and compliance
Manage maintenance and upkeep of company tools and equipment
Operate forklift to load, unload, and transport materials
Ensure safe transport practices, including proper load securing (straps, nets, tarps)

Operations & Administrative Support

Maintain accurate records of inventory, maintenance, and vendor activity
Support documentation related to safety, compliance, and operations
Assist with tracking labor, job activities, and operational data
Provide general administrative support, including expense tracking and basic IT coordination
Perform additional duties as assigned

Qualifications:

Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities
Experience in logistics, operations, inventory, or maintenance coordination preferred
Forklift experience or ability to become certified preferred
Attention to detail, particularly in invoice review and recordkeeping
Basic knowledge of vehicle and equipment maintenance practices
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Outlook, etc.)
Strong communication and problem-solving skills
Ability to work independently and collaboratively across teams
Familiarity with prevailing wage compliance and documentation is a plus

Minimum Requirements

2+ years of prior experience in logistics or coordination role
High School Diploma or equivalent
Clean driving record and valid driver's license
Able to work in inclement weather and dirty conditions
Dairy farm background a plus
Drug-free, including Cannabis - must be able to pass drug test and be part of random testing program
Regenis' work is safety-sensitive.
Solid work ethic
Learning and growth mindset

Applicants for this position must be able to produce a negative drug test.

Working Conditions

The position works both in an office setting and on site
Office work involves remaining in a stationary position in front of a computer
Possible exposure to hazardous materials and/or gasses
PPE and training provided
Occasional overtime
Must be able to climb ladders and stairs

Location

This position will be based at the Company’s Jerome, Idaho location.

How to Apply

Submit your application and resume via the Regenis website: regenis.net/careers

EEO Statement

Regenis is an Equal Opportunity Employer and welcomes different perspectives. We recognize that the best way we can serve our diverse communities is to have those communities represented in our workforce. Regenis doesn't discriminate against, nor tolerate harassment by any person, including co-workers, supervisors, or third parties, based on any Protected Characteristics. Creating an inclusive and professional environment where employees feel comfortable, safe, and free from inappropriate and disrespectful conduct allows our employees to flourish and effectively collaborate. We take policy violations very seriously at all stages of the employment lifecycle. If you feel that you have been discriminated against in the application or hiring process, please contact us so that we can resolve any issues.

Disclaimer

Applicants may be subject to a background check. Employees in this position must be able to satisfactorily perform the essential functions of the position. If requested, Regenis will make every effort to provide reasonable accommodations to enable employees with disabilities to perform the position’s essential job duties. As markets change and the Company grows, job descriptions may change over time as requirements and employee skill levels evolve. With this understanding, Regenis retains the right to change or assign other duties to this Operations and Maintenance Technician position.

Application Deadline

Regenis accepts applications on a rolling basis.

Even if your qualifications don’t match our criteria exactly, we welcome you to apply if you have an interest and feel you would be able to succeed in this position. We have found that people with a diversity of backgrounds and variety of experiences in our workforce creates a more rewarding, dynamic, and effective work life.

positionName: Property Management Coordinator – Short-Term Rental Operations (Entry-Level / Real Estate Experience
description:
Job Summary

We’re looking for a motivated, detail-oriented team member to help manage our growing portfolio of luxury short-term rental cabins. This is an *excellent entry-level opportunity* — almost like a paid internship — especially for *University of Tennessee at Knoxville (UTK)* students or recent grads who want hands-on experience in property management, guest services, and real estate. You’ll work directly with property owners (many are top investors in the Smokies), guests, and our small, tight-knit team. Expect occasional on-site visits (1–2 times per week) to beautiful mountain properties — not a bad way to spend a workday!

Key Responsibilities

* *Onboard new properties/clients* — coordinate photography uploads, update Airbnb listings and descriptions, and ensure everything is guest-ready.
* *Guest communication* — respond promptly via messaging and get on the phone to resolve issues quickly and professionally.
* *Maintenance requests* — document issues thoroughly, send them to our handyman with all necessary details, and submit proper paperwork for accounting.
* *Airbnb platform management* — monitor listings, optimize descriptions/photos, and work to overturn negative reviews or unfavorable rulings.
* *Software oversight* — daily monitoring of our property management platform to keep everything running smoothly.
* *In-person support* — occasionally meet owners at properties for onboarding or maintenance coordination (properties are primarily in Gatlinburg, Pigeon Forge, Wears Valley, and surrounding Smoky Mountain areas).

Pay: $15.00 - $16.00 per hour

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Sevier County, TN

positionName: Coordinator, Business Operations I- Bali Hai, Princeville, Kauai
description:
We Put the World on Vacation

Travel + Leisure Co. is the world’s leading vacation ownership and travel membership company, with a dynamic and growing portfolio of resort, travel club, and lifestyle travel brands. Our dedicated associates help the company achieve its mission to put the world on vacation. Innovation and growth keep our work interesting and fun. Every day is a chance to learn something new and turn vacation inspiration into exceptional experiences for millions of travelers worldwide.

As a Business Operations Coordinator for Wyndham Destinations this person will be responsible for delivering quality support for our Sales and Marketing processes.

How You'll Shine:

Provide exceptional customer service to our guests, business partners, and team
Identify ways in which to improve our customer service or processes
Collect and/or validate data when interacting with our guests to ensure they receive the best experience with us
Manage and protect company assets through all interactions
Understand and implement operational procedures that govern our business
Support new initiatives and help teams that are impacted by these changes
Maintain daily, weekly, monthly, or quarterly reporting needs
Successfully leverage your resources (your team, leadership, procedural guides, etc.) to provide quick support to questions as they arise

What You'll Bring:

Validating data and reporting or answering questions from onsite business partners
Supporting the integration of new processes and procedures
Proficient in Microsoft Suite
Have availability to support the business throughout weekends and holidays
Experience delivering outstanding customer service, with excitement!

How You'll Be Rewarded:

We offer a diverse range of comprehensive health and welfare benefits to associates who work 30 or more hours per week to meet your needs and support you throughout your career with us. Travel + Leisure Co. benefits include: Note: Temporary and/or seasonal associates are ineligible for Paid Time Off.
• Medical
• Dental
• Vision
• Flexible spending accounts
• Life and accident coverage
• Disability
• Depending on position, paid time off, parental leave and holidays (speak to your recruiter for additional information)
• Wish day paid time to volunteer at an approved organization of your choice
• 401k with employer match (subject to eligibility requirements, including tenure - speak to your recruiter for additional information)
• Legal and identity theft plan
• Voluntary income protection benefits
• Wellness program (subject to provider availability)
• Employee Assistance Program

Compensation

Generally starting at $22.02 - $22.48 per hour. The actual hourly rate of the candidate(s) selected for this role will be set based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to experience, education, skills, training, and work location.

Where Memories Start with You

Hospitality is at the heart of all we do at Travel + Leisure Co. Here, you’ll find an inclusive environment where we deliver excellence and take time to have fun, celebrate together, and support one another. We're always looking ahead to what’s next and how we can strengthen our business, its neighboring communities, and the customer experience. Join our global team and build a career where memories start with you.

We are an equal opportunity employer, and all applications will be considered for employment without attention to their membership in any protected class. If you require any reasonable accommodation to complete your application or any part of the recruiting process, please email your request to MyCareer@travelandleisure.com, including the title and location of the position for which you are applying.

As a Business Operations Coordinator for Wyndham Destinations this person will be responsible for delivering quality support for our Sales and Marketing processes.

How You'll Shine:

  • Provide exceptional customer service to our guests, business partners, and team
  • Identify ways in which to improve our customer service or processes
  • Collect and/or validate data when interacting with our guests to ensure they receive the best experience with us
  • Manage and protect company assets through all interactions
  • Understand and implement operational procedures that govern our business
  • Support new initiatives and help teams that are impacted by these changes
  • Maintain daily, weekly, monthly, or quarterly reporting needs
  • Successfully leverage your resources (your team, leadership, procedural guides, etc.) to provide quick support to questions as they arise

What You'll Bring:

  • Validating data and reporting or answering questions from onsite business partners
  • Supporting the integration of new processes and procedures
  • Proficient in Microsoft Suite
  • Have availability to support the business throughout weekends and holidays
  • Experience delivering outstanding customer service, with excitement!

How You'll Be Rewarded:

We offer a diverse range of comprehensive health and welfare benefits to associates who work 30 or more hours per week to meet your needs and support you throughout your career with us. Travel + Leisure Co. benefits include: Note: Temporary and/or seasonal associates are ineligible for Paid Time Off.
  • Medical
  • Dental
  • Vision
  • Flexible spending accounts
  • Life and accident coverage
  • Disability
  • Depending on position, paid time off, parental leave and holidays (speak to your recruiter for additional information)
  • Wish day paid time to volunteer at an approved organization of your choice
  • 401k with employer match (subject to eligibility requirements, including tenure - speak to your recruiter for additional information)
  • Legal and identity theft plan
  • Voluntary income protection benefits
  • Wellness program (subject to provider availability)
  • Employee Assistance Program

Compensation

Generally starting at $22.02 - $22.48 per hour. The actual hourly rate of the candidate(s) selected for this role will be set based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to experience, education, skills, training, and work location.

Where Memories Start with You

Hospitality is at the heart of all we do at Travel + Leisure Co. Here, you’ll find an inclusive environment where we deliver excellence and take time to have fun, celebrate together, and support one another. We're always looking ahead to what’s next and how we can strengthen our business, its neighboring communities, and the customer experience. Join our global team and build a career where memories start with you.

We are an equal opportunity employer, and all applications will be considered for employment without attention to their membership in any protected class. If you require any reasonable accommodation to complete your application or any part of the recruiting process, please email your request to MyCareer@travelandleisure.com, including the title and location of the position for which you are applying.

description:
*The Role*

We're looking for a *Fulfillment & Operations Coordinator* to own the day-to-day backend of our e-commerce and warehouse operations. You'll run the full flow from order intake to shipment, returns, inventory, and warehouse upkeep and make sure every piece is handled with care and accuracy.

We think of the job in terms of outcomes, not checklists. Done means:.

* All daily DTC and wholesale orders are picked, packed, and shipped accurately
* Any order issues, holds, or exceptions are reviewed and handled or clearly escalated
* Inbound deliveries that arrived that day are received, checked in, and put away
* Inventory counts are updated, checked, and reconciled as part of the daily flow
* Back-stock is moved forward, pick bins are refilled, and packing stations are restocked
* Returns, exchanges, and customer inquiries are processed within expected timelines
* Boxes are broken down, trash is taken out, and the warehouse is clean, stocked, organized, and ready for the next day

This is a role for someone who takes pride in their work, cares about the details, and sees things through. We measure success by outcomes, accuracy, and follow through.

What You'll Own

*Daily fulfillment*

* Create shipping labels and related documentation
* Pick, pack, and ship daily DTC and wholesale orders accurately and on time
* Follow wholesale-specific packing requirements (labeling, packing slips, carton counts, retailer compliance)
* Review order issues including holds, cancellations, address changes, and stock problems
* Ensure orders move out same day or next business day when expected

*Returns, exchanges & customer service*

* Process returns, exchanges, and replacements
* Respond to customer service inquiries related to orders, shipping, and fulfillment
* Flag recurring issues, errors, or customer pain points so they can be fixed at the source

*Inbound & inventory*

* Receive and intake inbound inventory and deliveries accurately, the day they arrive
* Update and reconcile inventory counts daily as part of the normal workflow
* Run weekly cycle counts and support full inventory audits
* Move back-stock forward, refill pick bins, and keep active inventory ready
* Report discrepancies, low stock, or operational issues early

*Warehouse & systems*

* Keep packing stations stocked, organized, and fully operational
* Break down shipping and inbound boxes, take out trash, and keep the space free of clutter throughout the day
* Keep the warehouse clean, organized, and operationally efficient
* Track shipments, inventory issues, and supply needs
* Create clear end-of-day summaries covering unresolved issues, holds, returns, rejected items, and reorders needed
* Help improve workflows, documentation, and systems to increase speed, accuracy, and accountability

*How Success Is Measured*

You'll be measured on order accuracy, on-time shipping, inventory accuracy, and how quickly returns and customer messages get handled. We share these numbers openly and review them together so you always know where you stand.

Who We're Looking For

*Required*

* 1+ year of experience in fulfillment, warehouse operations, e-commerce operations, logistics, or a similar operations role
* Experience with shipping platforms such as TikTok Shipping, Shippo, Shopify, or similar tools.
* Comfortable with inventory systems, spreadsheets, and reporting tools
* Strong attention to detail and accuracy
* Clear written communication for customer service and internal reporting
* Able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-moving environment
* Able to lift and move heavy boxes (30lbs) with or without reasonable accommodation
* Reliable, consistent, and punctual

*How we know you're the right person*

* You take pride in finishing what you start and leaving things better than you found them
* You have a sharp eye for details and notice things others miss
* You own your work, the wins and the mistakes, and move quickly to fix what needs fixing
* You communicate clearly and early, especially when something needs attention
* You like a clean, organized space and take satisfaction in keeping it that way
* You're proactive, dependable, and the kind of person a small team can count on

*Bonus Points*

* Wholesale or retail fulfillment experience
* Interest in alternative, goth, Y2K, Alt, or spooky fashion culture
* Curiosity about our products, brand aesthetic, and community
* Desire to learn about the culture behind the brand to better support customers and operations
* Passion for building and improving systems in a creative, fast-growing business
* Enjoys working in a creative, fast-moving environment

*For faster interview consideration, you can submit a short video intro here:*
https://www.videoask.com/frcjtl2k5

*In your video, please answer these 3 questions:*

* What experience do you have in fulfillment, warehouse, or e-commerce operations, and what were you personally responsible for?
* Tell us about a time you caught a problem, mistake, or loose end before it became a bigger issue. What did you do?
* Wicked Misfit is a fast-paced alternative fashion brand with a gothic, edgy aesthetic. What stands out to you about our brand, and why do you feel like you’d be a strong fit for this environment?

Pay: $20.00 - $23.00 per hour

Expected hours: 30.0 – 40.0 per week

Benefits:
* Employee discount



Ability to Commute:
* Arcadia, CA 91006 (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

description:
*Job Summary*
We are looking for someone who thrives on adaptability and precision. This role combines executive-level support with operational oversight across HR, accounting, and treasury functions. Reporting directly to the Executive Assistant, your responsibilities are split into two main categories:

* *Routine:* Consistently managing a core set of daily/weekly tasks (deposits, payment processing, and administrative tracking).
* *Ad Hoc:* Executing projects assigned by the EA based on management’s current needs, such as drafting staff memos, conducting research, or resolving vendor issues.

Because your priorities will shift based on what management needs resolved, being able to pivot while maintaining follow-through is vital. You will also provide front desk coverage as needed to support clinic operations directly.

_*Travel Requirements:*_ _Applicant must be able to commute to our clinics in Waipahu, Wahiawā, and Waiʻanae based on operational needs._

*Responsibilities*

* *Operational Support:* Assist the Executive Assistant with day-to-day tasks, leadership directives, and special projects.
* *Routine Tasks:* Consistently manage recurring responsibilities including, but not limited to: Processing bank deposits and credit card/virtual payments, administrative support for weekly front desk meetings (Thursdays), reviewing timecards, and basic administrative tracking and data entry.
* *Payroll & HR Admin:* Verify employee timesheets for accuracy and assist with payroll-related tasks or basic HR documentation.
* *Billing & Documentation:* Support billing workflows (billing experience is a plus) and manage digital/physical filing systems (scanning, uploading, and organizing documents).
* *Vendor Coordination:* Act as a point of contact for utilities and service providers to resolve issues or coordinate authorizations.
* *Clinic Support:* Provide front desk coverage and operational support during busy periods or for staff call-outs.

*How This Role Works (The "Standard Day")*

Success in this position is measured by *reliability and results.* While no two days will be exactly the same, but your workflow will typically look like this:

* *Routine Responsibilities:* You must complete your core recurring tasks (deposits, payments, etc.) accurately and on time.
* *Ad Hoc Tasks:* You will be assigned additional tasks throughout the day (e.g., drafting a memo, calling a vendor to resolve a billing dispute, etc.) that you must complete as soon as possible.
* *Priority Ad Hoc Tasks: *Some tasks may be identified as high priority. When that happens, they should be handled first and take precedence over other work.
* *Ownership*: You will have flexibility in how you approach and complete your work. As long as high priority tasks are handled first and all other work is completed accurately and on time; We trust you to manage your workload and keep tasks moving forward without the need for constant follow-up.

*What We’re Looking For*

* *Reliability & Ownership:* When a task is assigned, you own it from start to finish. Once you have the tools and training you need, we want to be able to "set it and forget it," trusting that the work is moving forward correctly without needing constant reminders or "nudges." You take full responsibility for the final result.
* *Resourceful Communication:* You are independent but not silent. While you try to find answers and problem-solve on your own first, you know exactly when to "raise a flag." We are happy to provide hands-on training, but we expect you to be vocal and communicate early if you are truly stuck or see something about to fall through the cracks.
* *Attention to Detail:* High accuracy is a non-negotiable requirement. You naturally double-check your work, especially when handling money, employee timesheets, and sensitive data.
* *Prioritization & Pivot Skills:* You’re able to switch gears when needed and adjust priorities throughout the day, especially when something urgent/ a “High Importance” item comes up.

*Preferred (Not Required)*

* Bachelor’s degree in Business or a related field.
* Experience with medical billing, insurance, or healthcare administration.
* *3+ years *of experience in operations support, office administration, or a similar role.

*Why This Role Is a Good Fit*

This is a great opportunity for someone who wants to learn how operations actually run behind the scenes and gain hands-on experience working directly with leadership in a dynamic role in a fast-paced healthcare environment.

*Growth Opportunity*
This role will begin as a contract position with an initial evaluation period of approximately three months. Individuals who demonstrate strong performance, reliability, and ownership during this period will be considered for full-time conversion. Full-time employees are eligible for benefits including health insurance, paid time off, a 401(k) plan, and other company-sponsored benefits.

Pay: $20.00 - $25.00 per hour

Benefits:
* Employee discount



Application Question(s):
* What is your desired rate of pay?

Work Location: In person
