description:
*Operations Coordinator*

*Your Opportunity: *

You have the opportunity to be surrounded by, learn from and grow with the top 1% of real estate professionals in North America. Come grow with us as our Operations Coordinator. We have a proven path and will provide you with the models, systems, tools, coaching, training, and accountability that ensure success. Show up with the will and commitment to succeed. In addition to creating a limitless growth path for your career, you also can earn opportunities for revenue share, growth share, vesting, investing, and earning an opportunity to become a part of our consulting team.

*Who Develops You: *Team Operator

*What You’re Great At: *

You are an organized, methodical, and driven professional with a servant leader's heart. Systems are your jam -- creating them, implementing them, refining them. Your attention to detail is unsurpassed and you thrive on your high standards, quality of work, and your ability to serve others. You are an empire protector focused on protecting the time and resources of yourself and the rest of the team.

*What You’ll Do: *

* Build, implement, maintain, and OWN all systems for delivering excellent internal and external customer service, internal communication, team calendar, company social media presence internally and externally, office management, client care systems, team retention and recognition including events, and referrals.
* Support the team growth efforts through posting recruiting ads, calling agent recruits, and scheduling appointments for Team Operator.
* Create and own systems for maintaining integrity of the database, including holding agents accountable to activities.
* Own the agent onboarding process, including holding trainings in person and virtually.
* Follow the systems for our productivity rhythms to ensure agent productivity.
* Plan and execute events for clients that are done at a high level to drive business referrals.
* Ensure all team receipts are obtained and submit to the finance team, when reports are generated, take ownership of finding opportunities to reduce expenses.
* Create and maintain the team’s operations manual that documents all systems and standards that are not able to exist within our technology leverage, Brivity.
* Keep the team leader informed of any challenges that arise and bring potential solutions.
* Commit to weekly 1:1 coaching sessions, PLACE trainings, and Brivity trainings.
* Provide a world-class experience for clients through phone and email on behalf of the team through contract and beyond.
* Manage the listing launch and contract to close process for all transactions, including overseeing a virtual assistant that completes most of the process.
* Coordinate marketing, photography, staging, sign installation, and other listing launch functions.
* Act as the liaison between real estate agents, clients, attorneys, escrow companies, and mortgage brokers.
* Generate reviews and referrals from clients during and after the transaction.

*Skills and Character Necessary: *

* Notice and take pride in the details
* Team player
* Proven ability to lead yourself and others with conviction
* Self-motivated
* Owning projects once asked to do them and following up with status updates
* Ability and desire to focus on one task for at least an hour during time blocks
* Positive and direct communication, both written and verbal
* Ability to problem solve and use critical thinking daily
* Have a “do whatever it takes” mentality
* Follow a current process as well as improving them and create new processes
* Learning-based and growth-minded
* Proactive vs. reactive
* Impeccably organized
* Knowledge of basic office suite software and calendaring systems
* Excellent time management skills
* Concerned about doing things the right way
* Calm and motivated under pressure
* Have patience with a high volume, detailed role
* Use social media for the benefit of the company as an ambassador
* Flexible

*Required Background: *

* Requires a prior work history that demonstrates the ability to plug into and improve existing systems and provide an enthusiastic level of support to a team
* 1+ years experience in an operations/administrative role
* Real estate license preferred but not required

*Other Details: *

* Compensation depending on experience, $30 per hour
* We have an in-house work model, Monday through Friday, occasional nights and weekends for planned events
* This is for a part-time position, about 20 hours per week, that could lead to full time 40 hours
* We are excited to meet the person(s) that truly believe they could be a match!

Job Type: Part-time

Pay: $30.00 per hour



Application Question(s):
* Do you have any experience in the real estate industry?

License/Certification:
* Washington real estate license (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

description:
Title: *Mission Support & Operations Coordinator*

Location: Central Virginia / Charlottesville, VA metro area (Madison, VA)

ARiA applies broad interdisciplinary expertise in acoustics, machine learning, and signal processing toward innovative R&D for government and corporate clients. As a high-impact, 20-person prime contractor, _ARiA is a small business where you can make a big difference_. We solve critical challenges—from helping the Navy use simulation to train sonar operators to working with the Air Force to transform technology acquisition through AI. We offer the technical autonomy of a startup with the mission-critical stability of a defense leader.

*ARiA: Real Research. Real World.TM*

For more information, visit www.ariacoustics.com.

*Position Description*

ARiA is seeking a highly motivated, low-ego Mission Support & Operations Coordinator to serve as the operational catalyst for the organization. Based at our Central Virginia headquarters, this role allows you to trade the stress of a long city commute for a high-impact career in a scenic rural setting with a short, stress-free drive.

This is a collaborative facilitator role designed to synchronize our accounting, security, and technical teams. We are looking for an “Integrator" who views meticulous documentation and regulatory filings as a strategic service that protects the firm’s growth and ensures the team's success. Success at ARiA is measured by your ability to create "frictionless compliance," allowing our scientists to stay focused on world-class innovation. You must possess the intellectual humility to partner with technical staff, the resourcefulness to solve complex administrative puzzles independently, and a proactive mindset that supports the team by making administrative tasks as effortless as possible

_Responsibilities:_

* Operational Integration: Proactively synchronize the contract lifecycle by tracking government filings, cost vouchers, and technical deliverables through successful completion.
* Protective Compliance: Serve as a steward of the company’s regulatory standing by managing representations, certifications, and policies, so that the team’s research is always audit-ready and protected.
* Team Enablement & Culture: Facilitate a seamless experience for staff by coordinating the hiring process, managing benefits, and leading and welcoming onboarding journey for new team members.
* Resource Stewardship: Maintain a high-performance office environment at the Madison headquarters by overseeing vendors, managing asset inventories, and ensuring researchers have the resources they need to excel.
* Administrative Synchronization: Act as a resource for the technical team regarding travel and expenses, streamlining these processes to ensure compliance while minimizing the administrative burden on technical staff.

*Requirements*

* United States Citizenship and eligibility for a government security investigation are mandatory.
* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience.
* High Agency: A self-starter who thrives on technical autonomy and takes pride in delivering excellence without constant supervision.
* Solution-Oriented Partnership: Demonstrated ability to work closely with technical and executive staff, using a collaborative approach to solve problems.
* Digital Fluency: Expert proficiency with Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and modern cloud-based software tools.
* Precision Communication: Exceptional attention to detail and a professional, helpful manner in all internal and external communications.

*Desirable Skills, Qualifications, and Experience*

* Prior experience with DoD contracting standards and government regulation management.
* A history of streamlining operational workflows to improve organizational velocity and team satisfaction.

*To apply submit cover letter and resume*

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $65,000.00 - $85,000.00 per year

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Employee assistance program
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Parental leave
* Professional development assistance
* Relocation assistance
* Retirement plan



Application Question(s):
* This position requires compliance with Federal drug laws, even if such substances are legal in your state. Will you comply with Federal drug laws?
* Due to the nature of the information required to perform this job, U.S. Citizenship is required for this position. Are you a citizen of the United States?

Location:
* Madison, VA 22727 (Preferred)

Ability to Commute:
* Madison, VA 22727 (Required)

Work Location: In person

description:
This role is responsible for driving administrative efficiency across leasing, property management, and accounting functions. The position ensures documentation integrity, financial record accuracy, and operational compliance while supporting the day-to-day administrative infrastructure of real estate operations.

The ideal candidate brings experience working in a *real estate, property management, or accounting environment*, understands the importance of accurate financial documentation, and can maintain structured systems in a fast-paced operational setting.

*Key Responsibilities*

*Document & Records Management*

* Maintain and continuously improve *physical and digital filing systems for tenant, lease, and financial documentation* to ensure accuracy, accessibility, and regulatory compliance.
* Implement standardized *document naming, indexing, and retention protocols* for leases, amendments, tenant correspondence, invoices, and accounting records.
* Organize and prepare documentation for *internal audits, financial reviews, and property-level reporting*.
* Ensure all tenant files remain *complete, compliant, and audit-ready*.

*Financial Documentation & Accounting Support*

* Scan, code, and electronically file *rent payments, tenant receipts, and miscellaneous income documentation*.
* Assist the accounting department with *invoice documentation, payment support, and transaction backup files*.
* Support the reconciliation process by ensuring *accurate and complete financial documentation* is available for accounting workflows.
* Identify discrepancies between *lease terms, tenant payments, and recorded transactions*, escalating issues as needed.

*Leasing & Property Operations Support*

* Support leasing operations with *lease documentation preparation, tenant file management, and compliance tracking*.
* Maintain organized records for *lease agreements, renewals, amendments, certificates, and tenant communications*.
* Coordinate with property management and accounting teams to ensure *lease data aligns with financial records and reporting requirements*.
* Assist with document preparation for *tenant onboarding, renewals, and property administration processes*.

*Operational Efficiency & Special Projects*

* Identify inefficiencies within *documentation, leasing administration, and accounting support workflows*, recommending process improvements.
* Assist with *data organization, system migrations, and property management software updates*.
* Support cross-department operational initiatives and special projects with minimal supervision.

*Qualifications*

* *2+ years of administrative experience*, preferably within *real estate, property management, or an accounting/finance department*.
* Experience working with *leases, tenant documentation, invoices, financial records, or property management documentation* strongly preferred.
* Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage *high-volume real estate and financial documentation*.
* Exceptional attention to detail and commitment to *financial and operational accuracy*.
* Proficiency in *Microsoft Excel* (sorting, filtering, basic formulas) and *Microsoft Word*.
* Experience with *property management software, accounting systems, or document management platforms* is a plus.
* Ability to handle *confidential tenant and financial information with discretion*.
* Self-starter with strong prioritization skills in a *deadline-driven operational environment*.

*Performance Indicators*

Success in this role will be measured by:

* Maintaining *100% accurate, compliant, and audit-ready tenant and financial filing systems*.
* Timely processing and digital storage of *rent payments, invoices, and financial documentation*.
* Reduced administrative backlog and *faster document retrieval across leasing and accounting teams*.
* Zero compliance or documentation gaps in *tenant, lease, or financial records*.

Pay: $25.00 - $26.00 per hour

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

description:
Precision Electronic Glass (PEG), a family-owned business since 1962, specializes in crafting high-quality custom glass and quartz components for a variety of advanced industries, including medical devices, aerospace, semiconductor manufacturing, analytical instrumentation, fiber optics, and scientific research equipment. Many of the components we produce are used in critical applications where tight tolerances, visual quality, and dimensional accuracy are essential.

The Administrative Operations Coordinator functions as a central coordination point for administrative and operational activities, supporting HR processes and internal systems management. This role is responsible for ensuring consistency, organization, and efficiency across day to day operations, while maintaining compliance with ISO standards. By partnering with internal stakeholders and external vendors, the position helps drive streamlined processes, supports workforce and systems coordination, and contributes to the overall effectiveness of the organization.

This position reports directly to the VP of Human Resources.

*Duties and Responsibilities*

*Administrative Support*

* Coordinates general administrative and office operations to support daily business needs
* Answers incoming phone calls and front door, directs inquiries appropriately
* Serves as a central point of contact for internal administrative requests and follows through to resolution
* Maintains and organizes company documents, templates, and shared files to ensure version control and accessibility
* Pulls data from sources such as ERP system and HRIS software and prepares spreadsheets, reports, and presentations
* Prints, organizes, and distributes documentation as needed
* Identifies opportunities to improve administrative and operational processes and supports implementation of improvements

*Human Resources Support*

* Assists with hiring process: recruiting, applicant tracking, scheduling interviews
* Assists with onboarding logistics: workspace setup, system access coordination, and new hire materials
* Assists with employee training: scheduling, documentation, and executing training topics
* Maintains employee records and documentation in accordance with company policies and compliance requirements
* Supports administration of HR programs and initiatives, including engagement efforts and internal communications
* Assists in maintaining confidentiality of employee information and sensitive HR data

*IT & Systems Support*

* Provides basic internal IT support such as password resets
* Assists employees with resolving basic computer and system issues
* Communicates and coordinates with external IT providers

*Other *

* Manages Safety Committee
* Manages employee’s association
* Maintains a neat and safe work environment
* Other duties as directed and assigned by manager or other department leads

*Knowledge, Skills, & Abilities Required*

* Ability to work with basic numerical data, including tracking, reconciliation, and reporting
* Excellent interpersonal and negotiation skills with a proven ability to create and maintain positive working relationships with staff, vendors, suppliers, shippers, and customers
* Strong organizational and time management skills with the ability to prioritize competing tasks and meet deadlines
* Possesses problem-solving, active listening, critical thinking, and decision-making abilities
* Possesses substantial knowledge of computerized systems including Microsoft Office Suite, Visual Manufacturing, and internet search activities
* Ability to keep close attention to detail and multi-task
* Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion and sound judgment
* Possesses excellent verbal and written communication skills
* Ability to adapt to changing priorities and business needs in a fast paced environment
* Possesses an understanding of record-keeping practices in compliance with ISO 9001 standards
* Ability to read, write, speak and understand English to perform the duties of the job

*Experience Requirements*

* Minimum 2 years of experience in an administrative, office support, or operations support role required
* Experience in a manufacturing environment strongly preferred
* Experience with manufacturing software (Visual Manufacturing or similar ERP/MRP system) preferred
* Experience supporting basic IT functions (password resets, troubleshooting, working with third-party IT providers) preferred
* Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office products including Excel (spreadsheets, formulas, data organization), Outlook, Word, and PowerPoint required
* Experience preparing reports, spreadsheets, and presentations for management
* Experience working within ISO 9001 or other quality systems preferred

*Supervisory Responsibility*

This position has no direct supervisory responsibilities.

*Physical Demands & Work Environment*

While performing the duties of this position, the employee will regularly sit, use a computer, and communicate with others. The role also requires occasional standing, walking, bending, kneeling, and reaching.

The employee must be able to use hands for fine motor tasks such as typing and handling documents. Occasional lifting and carrying of items up to 25 pounds may be required. The work environment includes both office settings and exposure to operational areas of the facility.

*Position Type and Expected Hours of Work*

This is a full-time position. Flex scheduling is available upon request but must fit the needs of the company.

*Travel*

The employee may need to travel locally for training, vendor visits, or to pick up and deliver materials. *Reliable transportation and the ability to use a personal vehicle for local travel are required.*

*Our Interview Process*

We review each application based on experience, reliability, and overall fit for the position. If your background aligns with what we are seeking, we will contact you to schedule a phone interview.

*Phone Interview*

During the phone interview, we will:

* Provide a brief overview of PEG and what we do
* Discuss the open position
* Review your work history and experience
* Answer any initial questions you may have

*In-Person Interview*

If selected to move forward, you’ll be invited onsite to:

* Tour portions of our facility (when applicable)
* Meet team members
* You will be introduced to the position and may be asked to process or review certain sample parts to give you a realistic idea of the job and the type of work performed at PEG. Please note: This is not production work and you will not be performing billable or customer work. The purpose is simply to give you hands-on exposure to the type of tasks involved and to help both you and PEG determine if the role is a good fit.

You will also complete a short aptitude assessment that includes:

* Reading comprehension
* Basic math (addition and subtraction)
* Knowledge of MS Word & Excel

*References*

* Candidates must provide at least one professional reference from a current or former supervisor.
* The reference should be someone who can speak to your work performance, reliability, and overall job responsibilities.

*Post-Offer Requirements*

If you are offered a position, employment will be contingent upon:

* Completing a physical and drug screening within 48 hours of the offer
* This position is subject to a background check as a condition of employment.
* Verification of eligibility to work in the United States through E-Verify

*Job Type*: Full-time

*Work Location*: In person

Pay: $20.00 - $25.00 per hour

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Employee assistance program
* Flexible schedule
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Tuition reimbursement
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

Precision Electronic Glass (PEG), a family-owned business since 1962, specializes in crafting high-quality custom glass and quartz components for a variety of advanced industries, including medical devices, aerospace, semiconductor manufacturing, analytical instrumentation, fiber optics, and scientific research equipment. Many of the components we produce are used in critical applications where tight tolerances, visual quality, and dimensional accuracy are essential.

The Administrative Operations Coordinator functions as a central coordination point for administrative and operational activities, supporting HR processes and internal systems management. This role is responsible for ensuring consistency, organization, and efficiency across day to day operations, while maintaining compliance with ISO standards. By partnering with internal stakeholders and external vendors, the position helps drive streamlined processes, supports workforce and systems coordination, and contributes to the overall effectiveness of the organization.

This position reports directly to the VP of Human Resources.

  • Answers incoming phone calls and front door, directs inquiries appropriately

  • Maintains and organizes company documents, templates, and shared files to ensure version control and accessibility
  • Pulls data from sources such as ERP system and HRIS software and prepares spreadsheets, reports, and presentations
  • Prints, organizes, and distributes documentation as needed
  • Identifies opportunities to improve administrative and operational processes and supports implementation of improvements

Human Resources Support

  • Assists with hiring process: recruiting, applicant tracking, scheduling interviews
  • Assists with onboarding logistics: workspace setup, system access coordination, and new hire materials
  • Assists with employee training: scheduling, documentation, and executing training topics
  • Maintains employee records and documentation in accordance with company policies and compliance requirements
  • Supports administration of HR programs and initiatives, including engagement efforts and internal communications
  • Assists in maintaining confidentiality of employee information and sensitive HR data

IT & Systems Support

  • Provides basic internal IT support such as password resets
  • Assists employees with resolving basic computer and system issues
  • Communicates and coordinates with external IT providers

Other

  • Manages employee’s association
  • Maintains a neat and safe work environment

  • Experience in a manufacturing environment strongly preferred

description:
*Program & Operations Coordinator*

_*Greater Lehigh Valley | Full-Time | Non-Exempt | $50,000 – $60,000*_

The SkillsUSA Council of the Greater Lehigh Valley is seeking a highly organized and mission-driven Program & Operations Coordinator to support programs that prepare students for successful careers and leadership in today’s workforce.

This role is central to the daily operations of the organization, supporting events, financial administration, membership, programs, and school engagement initiatives. The ideal candidate enjoys working behind the scenes to keep systems running smoothly, events organized, and stakeholders informed.

If you thrive in a fast-paced nonprofit environment and are passionate about supporting education, workforce development, and student success, we encourage you to apply.

*About the Organization*

The SkillsUSA Council – Greater Lehigh Valley connects students, educators, and industry partners to build strong career pathways and leadership opportunities for students in career and technical education programs across the region.

*Position Overview*

The Program & Operations Coordinator provides essential administrative and operational support to ensure the smooth execution of council programs, events, financial processes, and organizational initiatives.

Working closely with the Executive Director, this position plays a key role in coordinating events, maintaining financial records, supporting membership and sponsorship programs, and assisting with board and administrative operations.

*Key Responsibilities*

_*Event & Program Coordination*_

 Coordinate logistics for council events, conferences, competitions, and industry tours

 Maintain the organizational event calendar and manage event registrations

 Prepare event materials such as packets, signage, apparel, and supplies

 Provide on-site administrative support during events

_*School & Advisor Engagement*_

 Serve as a primary contact for Career & Technical Student Organization (CTSO) advisors

 Communicate events, deadlines, and program updates to participating schools

 Maintain advisor contact databases and support engagement across the region

_*Financial & Administrative Support*_

 Maintain financial records in QuickBooks under the direction of the Executive Director

 Process banking transactions, payroll reporting, and account reconciliations

 Assist with financial reporting and documentation

_*Membership & Sponsorship Support*_

 Maintain membership and sponsorship databases

 Process renewals, payments, and related documentation

 Track sponsorship agreements and ensure fulfillment of sponsor benefits

_*Governance & Organizational Support*_

 Coordinate board and committee meeting logistics

 Prepare meeting materials and record meeting minutes

 Support grant tracking and reporting deadlines

 Assist with website updates, marketing materials, and technology coordination

*Qualifications*

_*Required*_

 3–5 years of experience in administrative operations, financial recordkeeping, event coordination, or nonprofit program support

 Working knowledge of accounting principles and financial recordkeeping

 Experience with QuickBooks or similar accounting software

 Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines

 Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)

 Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 High attention to detail and strong follow-through

_*Preferred*_

 Experience coordinating events or conferences

 Experience working with nonprofits, schools, or workforce development programs

*Education*

 Associate’s degree or equivalent combination of education, training, and relevant work experience in business, accounting, nonprofit management, or a related field will be considered

*Working Conditions*

 Full-time, non-exempt position based in the Greater Lehigh Valley region

 Salary range: $50,000 – $60,000

 Occasional evening and weekend hours required for events

 Local travel within the Lehigh Valley area required

 Ability to lift and transport event materials (approximately 25–30 lbs) and stand during events

_Employment is contingent upon successful completion and maintenance of required background checks and clearances for working with students and schools._

Why Join SkillsUSA?

This is an opportunity to support programs that make a meaningful impact on students, educators, and local industries. You will work closely with education and workforce partners to help prepare the next generation of skilled professionals.

_Equal Opportunity Employer_

Pay: $50,000.00 - $60,000.00 per year

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* Paid time off

Work Location: In person
